name: ENERGY STAR Certified Heat Pumps

columns.name: Series Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: EER2 (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: The Energy Efficiency Ratio (EER2) is the ratio of the produced cooling effect of an air conditioner or heat pump to its net work input, expressed in Btu/watt-hour using 10 CFR part 430, Subpart B, Appendix M1 – Section 3.6 Heating mode tests for Different Types of Heat Pumps, Including Heating-Only Heat Pumps.

columns.name: SEER2 (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: The Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio (SEER2) is the ratio of the total heat removed from the conditioned space during the annual cooling season to the total electrical energy consumed by the air conditioner or heat pump during the same season, expressed in Btu/Wh using 10 CFR part 430, Subpart B, Appendix M1 – Section 3.6 Heating mode tests for Different Types of Heat Pumps, Including Heating-Only Heat Pumps.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: A Single Package unit is any central air conditioner that has all major assemblies enclosed in one cabinet. A Split System is any central air conditioner that has two separate assemblies, including an indoor coil and an outdoor coil.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Furnace Model Number
columns.description: Central air conditioners or heat pumps without an integrated blower fan will be tested with a furnace. Products are only ENERGY STAR certified in combination with the listed furnace.

columns.name: Indoor Unit Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler.

columns.name: Outdoor Unit Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler.

columns.name: Outdoor Unit Brand Name
columns.description: For independent coil manufacturers (ICMs), this is the manufacturer of the outdoor unit used with the independent coil. For system manufacturers, the indoor and outdoor manufacturer is the ENERGY STAR Partner.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: AHRI Reference Number
columns.description: This is the reference number assigned by AHRI for certification.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: Meets Peak Cooling Requirements
columns.description: Meets EER2 requirements for efficient cooling at 95F.

columns.name: ICM Outdoor Unit Manufacturer
columns.description: This is the manufacturer of the outdoor unit for combinations that include indoor units from Independent Coil Manufacturers.

columns.name: Manufacturer Type
columns.description: A system manufacturer manufactures all the major assemblies in an air-source heat pump and/or central air-conditioner. An independent coil manufacturer (ICM) manufactures only the indoor unit (coil) in a central air conditioner or air-source heat pump split system.

columns.name: DR Protocol
columns.description: Indicates which standardized protocol is used to send load management messages to the unit.

columns.name: Features Enabled by Secondary Communication Module/Device
Connected Capable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model meets optional connected criteria as defined in the specification, which may include functions helpful to the electrical grid (standardized signals and responses to manage energy use) and those helpful to consumers, such as scheduling and energy reporting.

columns.name: Cold Climate
columns.description: Indicates heat pumps that meet the ENERGY STAR cold climate criteria; cold climate heat pumps will provide better heating performance in low ambient temperatures, making these products an excellent choice for cold climate installations where the heat pump will be the sole heating source.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the type of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: Communication Hardware Architecture
columns.description: The Hardware Architecture describes the type of module/device within or external to the system needed for connection.

columns.name: Primary Communication Module/Device Brand Name
columns.description: An external communication module or device is required if communication hardware is not built into the unit.

columns.name: Features Enabled by Primary Communication Module/Device
columns.description: Features enabled by external communication modules/devices allow the system to provide user control and monitoring and act on external commands delivered remotely.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: This list/dataset includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Features Enabled by Tertiary Communication Module/Device
Is Broadband Internet Connection Needed for Demand Response?
Tax Credit Eligible
columns.description: Indicates product model is eligible for federal income tax credits available under the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Controls Verification Procedure (CVP) Test
columns.description: Indicates which CVP test was performed to confirm the performance of the controls at 5F operating as they would in a customer’s home.

columns.name: Compressor Staging
columns.description: Indicates whether the compressors supports changing heating and cooling capacity: single stage, two stages, more two stages, continuously variable.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: System can communicate using these protocols for sending messages and requests, some of which work wirelessly and some with wires (open standard media/network layer).

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for HVAC equipment include R-32 and R-454B.

columns.name: Installation Capabilities
columns.description: Capabilities identified to aid installers, some of which only apply to centrally ducted systems. These may include 1) Refrigerant charge verification, 2) Airflow measurement or external static pressure, 3) Blower fan power draw, 4) Test mode for manual measurements, 5) Automatic system discovery, and/or 6) Preprogrammed system tests.

columns.name: HSPF2 (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: The Heating Seasonal Performance Factor (HSPF2) is the total heating output of a central air-conditioning heat pump during its normal annual usage period for heating, expressed in Btus and divided by the total electric power input, expressed in Btu/Wh using  10 CFR part 430, Subpart B, Appendix M1 – Section 3.6 Heating mode tests for Different Types of Heat Pumps, Including Heating-Only Heat Pumps.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 6.1 or 6.2 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Air-Source Heat Pumps that are effective as of January 1, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/heating_cooling/heat_pumps_air_source/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Smart Thermostats

columns.name: Communication Method Other
columns.description: Protocols/Methods Thermostat can use to communicate external to the HVAC system

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Demand Response Product Variations
columns.description: Describes key changes from typical Thermostat Product Demand Response capabilities when used with this Thermostat.

columns.name: Thermostat Heating and Cooling Control Features
columns.description: Describes user tools to control Heating and Cooling systems in the home.

columns.name: Time to Enter Standby (s)
columns.description: Time between a user interaction, for example, key press, and CT Device entering automatic sleep mode.

columns.name: Static Temperature Accuracy (±°F)
columns.description: Lab tested accuracy of thermostat readout, compared with a reference sensor (⁰F).

columns.name: Family ID
columns.description: Used to link Services that can be used with several different Connected Thermostat Devices.

columns.name: Additional Thermostat Model Numbers
columns.description: Additional Models for Thermostat with equivalent lab test results, for example, variations in product finish.

columns.name: Service Model Number
columns.description: CT Service Model Number, assigned by service provider for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Service Brand Name
columns.description: Brand name of CT Service, assigned by service provider for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the CT product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the CT Product model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Demand Response Summary
columns.description: Describes the ability to communicate with Demand Response systems.

columns.name: Thermostat Communication Method
columns.description: Protocols/Methods Thermostat can use to communicate external to the HVAC system

columns.name: Other Heating and Cooling Control Features
columns.description: Describes user tools to control Heating and Cooling systems in the home.

columns.name: Standby Power (W)
columns.description: Average power consumption of CT Device + Service when in automatic sleep mode.

columns.name: Thermostat Model Number
columns.description: Model Number, assigned by manufacturer or private labeler for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Thermostat Model Name
columns.description: Model Name, assigned by manufacturer or private labeler for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Thermostat Brand Name
columns.description: Brand Name, assigned by manufacturer or private labeler for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Thermostat Brand Owner
columns.description: Manufacturer or Private Labeler of Thermostat.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This indicates for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Service Model Name
columns.description: CT Service Model Name, assigned by service provider for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 1.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Connected Thermostats that are effective as of December 23, 2016. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/heating_cooling/smart_thermostats/key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Commercial Dishwashers

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Booster Heater Idle Energy Rate for High Temp and Dual Sanitizing Machines) (kW)
columns.description: If the booster heater idle energy rate was able to be measured separately (it is not inseparable), then this represents the booster heater idle energy consumption in kW.

columns.name: Inseparable Booster Heater
columns.description: Describes if the dishwasher has a booster heater that is inseparable.  A booster heater is a water heater that raises the temperature of preheated water 40 to 80°F. The preheated water is supplied to the unit [booster heater] at temperatures that are typically between100 and 140°F. This booster heater can be either integral to the dishwasher, or externally connected. The booster heater (internal or external) energy consumption shall be measured and reported separately, if possible. However, if booster energy cannot be measured separately it will be included in the idle energy rate measurements.

columns.name: Idle Energy Rate for Low Temp and Dual Sanitizing Machines (kW)
columns.description: For all dishwasher types, the dishwasher is considered "in idle mode" when it is not actively running but is still powered on and ready to wash dishes at the required temperature. The idle energy rate is the rate of energy consumed by the dishwasher while “holding” or maintaining wash tank water at the thermostat(s) set point.

columns.name: Sanitation Method
columns.description: Describes the method for which the commercial dishwasher machine applies hot water or a chemical sanitizing solution for the purposes of cleaning dishware. Dual Sanitizing machines must meet both the high temp and low temp requirements.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: Designates the type of dishmachine model.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Water Consumption (gallons/hr)
columns.description: Metric that represents the amount of water, in gallons, that is consumed while rinsing and sanitizing dishware in one hour by the dishwashing machine. This water consumption metric is used for single tank flight type and multiple tank flight type machines.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Water Consumption (gallons/sq. ft.)
columns.description: Metric that represents the amount of water, in gallons, that is consumed per one square foot of rack area while rinsing and sanitizing dishware. This water consumption metric is used for pot, pan, and utensil machines.

columns.name: Washing Energy Consumption (kWh/rack) for High Temp and Dual Sanitizing Machines
columns.description: Metric that represents total washing energy, including any internal or external booster heater energy in addition to the tank, heat, motor, control, and any additional auxiliary energy, expressed in kWh/rack.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Hot Water Energy Offset (kWh/rack)
columns.description: If the dishwasher claims the hot water energy offset, this represents the hot water energy offset in kWh/rack, calculated with Equation 1 of the Specification.

columns.name: Hot Water Energy Offset Claimed
columns.description: Indicates if the product is claiming the Hot Water Energy Offset provided in Equation 1 of the Specification. The machine must meet the definition for Heat Recovery Machine in Section 1 of the Specification to claim this offset.

columns.name: Washing Energy Consumption (kWh/rack) for Low Temp and Dual Sanitizing Machines
columns.description: Metric that represents total washing energy, including any internal or external booster heater energy in addition to the tank, heat, motor, control, and any additional auxiliary energy, expressed in kWh/rack.

columns.name: Pot Pan Utensil Machine Wash Area (ft2)
columns.description: The wash area available for pot, pan, and utensil dishmachines.

columns.name: Energy Saver Mode
columns.description: An operational setting that is designed to reduce energy during idle mode through temporary shut-down of certain machine components (pumps or belt motors) or reduction of certain temperature set points.

columns.name: Energy Saver Mode Idle Rate (kW)
columns.description: If a dishwasher has an energy saver mode, this represents the measured Energy Saver Mode Idle Energy Rate.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Idle Energy Rate for High Temp and Dual Sanitizing Machines (kW)
columns.description: For all dishwasher types, the dishwasher is considered "in idle mode" when it is not actively running but is still powered on and ready to wash dishes at the required temperature. The idle energy rate is the rate of energy consumed by the dishwasher while “holding” or maintaining wash tank water at the thermostat(s) set point.

columns.name: Water Consumption (gallons/rack)
columns.description: Metric that represents the amount of water, in gallons, that is consumed while rinsing and sanitizing one rack of dishware. This water consumption metric is used to qualify under counter, single tank door, pot, pan, and utensil, single tank rack conveyor, and multiple tank rack conveyor machines.

columns.name: Heat Recovery Machine
columns.description:
"Warewashing equipment with heat recovery; a heat exchanger that recovers energy from other heat sources (i.e. waste water, exhaust duct) for the purpose of heating potable water. This includes but is not limited to drain water, wash compartment, and/or exhaust heat exchangers; and supplemental heat pumps. The equipment:
1) must use a minimum of 95% of water volume from the cold-water inlet for the wash and rinse cycle. The machine can have both cold-water and hot-water inlets, but a maximum of 5% of wash rinse cycle water shall come from the hot water; 
2) must have an inlet water temperature of 70 ± 3°F; and 
3) must operate at or above 180°F. "

columns.name: Heat Recovery Features
columns.description: The following types of energy heat recovery features are possible: Exhaust Heat Exchanger; Wash Compartment Heat Exchanger; Supplemental Heat Pump; Drain Water Heat Exchanger; Other Energy Recovery.

columns.name: Racks per Hour
columns.description: The racks per hour for this dishwasher.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 3.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Commercial Dishwashers that are effective as of July 27, 2021. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/commercial_food_service_equipment/commercial_dishwashers/key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Residential Clothes Washers

columns.name: Drum Capacity for the dryer in a Combination All-in-One Washer-Dryer
columns.description: For combination all-in-one washer-dryers, this is the drum capacity of the clothes dryers in cubic feet as measured by the U.S. Department of Energy test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Subpart B, Appendix D2. Products with a drum capacity less than 4.4 cubic feet are considered compact.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Energy Test Cycle Information for the dryer in a Combination All-in-One Washer-Dryer
columns.description: For combination all-in-one washer-dryers, the cycle selection and settings (temperature and dryness where separately selectable) as defined in the U.S. Department of Energy test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Subpart B, Appendix D2, Section 3.3, used for certification to ENERGY STAR.

columns.name: Combined Energy Factor (CEF) for the dryer in a Combination All-in-One Washer-Dryer
columns.description: CEF is the energy performance metric for ENERGY STAR clothes dryers; the higher the CEF the more efficient the clothes dryer. The metric has units lbs/kWh and is calculated by dividing the weight of the test load by the sum of the per cycle standby and off mode energy consumption and either the total per-cycle electricity dryer energy consumption or the total per-cycle gas dryer energy consumption expressed in kilowatt hours (kWh).

columns.name: US Federal Standard (IWF)
columns.description: The mandatory water efficiency standards for clothes washers, established by DOE rule-making activities and federal laws.

columns.name: Communication Standard Application Layer
columns.description: Open Standard - Application Layer include SEP 1.x, SEP2.x, OpenADR, etc.

columns.name: Direct on-premises Open-standard Based Interconnection
columns.description: Connected appliances may or may not enable open-standards connectivity in the home. Consider the following illustrative examples: "Yes" An appliance that can directly communicate using Wi-Fi and OpenADR, or wired Ethernet and SEP 2.x; "No" An appliance that uses proprietary communications to/from the cloud, and is capable of supporting open-standards communications only in the cloud.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what you need in your home to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control, e.g. ‘WiFi’ or Zigbee.

columns.name: Estimated Energy Test Cycle Time (min) for the dryer in a Combination All-in-One Washer-Dryer
columns.description: For combination all-in-one washer-dryers, the estimated energy test cycle time is the time taken to complete the test cycle necessary for ENERGY STAR certification. Products must complete the energy test cycle in less than 80 minutes in order to be certified as ENERGY STAR. The estimated test cycle time does not reflect actual consumer cycle times which vary as a result of load size, dampness, and composition.

columns.name: Heat Pump Technology for the dryer in a Combination All-in-One Washer-Dryer
columns.description: Highly energy efficient heat pump technology typically heats, dehumidifies and circulates air in the dryer drum, so no vent is required. A Heat Pump model solely uses heat pump technology to dry clothes, while a Hybrid Heat Pump model uses a combination of heat pump technology and traditional electric resistance heating technology.

columns.name: US Federal Standard (IMEF)
columns.description: The mandatory energy efficiency standards for clothes washers, established by DOE rulemaking activities and federal laws.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters used to identify models with the same performance characteristics. This sequence is generated by certification bodies and is reported as a single row of data on the lists of certified products.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Paired ENERGY STAR Clothes Dryer ENERGY STAR Model Identifier
columns.description: Paired clothes dryer ENERGY STAR Model Identifier.

columns.name: Connected
columns.description: Indicates whether the model offers advanced controls and increased functionality such as sending notifications when the washer is done or allowing for a service center to remotely diagnose problems. Clothes washers with connected functionality are smart grid ready; if linked with your energy provider or an energy management system these products can dynamically adjust their energy use, potentially lowering your electric bill. For combination all-in-one washer-dryer products the washer and dryer must meet connected criteria.

columns.name: Additional Dryer Features for the dryer in a Combination All-in-One Washer-Dryer
columns.description: For combination all-in-one washer-dryers, features applicable to the clothes dryer may include drum light, wrinkle prevention option, steam cycle, sanitization cycle, filter cleaning indicator, energy monitor/indicator, time remaining display, and supplementary drying system.

columns.name: Calculated Combined Energy Factor - Max Dryness Setting (lbs/kWh) for the dryer in a Combination All-in-One Washer-Dryer
columns.description: For combination all-in-one washer-dryers, the Calculated CEF – Max Dry is a measure of the dryer’s energy performance in a more energy consumptive setting, namely the normal cycle with the highest dryness setting as defined in the ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2017 criteria. The Calculated CEF – Max Dry should not be directly compared to the CEF field, which is tested using the medium dryness setting, and represents a rated rather than measured value. The CEF metric has units lbs/kWh and is calculated by dividing the weight of the test load by the sum of the per cycle standby and off mode energy consumption and either the total per-cycle electricity dryer energy consumption or the total per-cycle gas dryer energy consumption expressed in kilowatt hours (kWh).

columns.name: Estimated Annual Energy Use (kWh/yr) for the dryer in a Combination All-in-One Washer-Dryer
columns.description: For combination all-in-one washer-dryers, the estimated annual energy use is based on the Combined Energy Factor and an annual usage of 283 cycles per year, as referenced by the U.S. Department of Energy test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Subpart B, Appendix D2. Actual energy consumption will vary on your usage patterns, including how often your run the dryer, what cycle you select, and load size. NOTE: For gas dryers the estimated annual energy use is expressed in kilowatt hours to allow for comparison, and represents the equivalent multi-source energy use of the product (e.g., natural gas or propane typically expressed in British thermal units (Btu)).

columns.name: Annual Water Use (gallons/yr)
columns.description: This is the estimated annual water use of the washer under typical conditions. It is based on an annual usage of 295 loads per year, or around 6 loads per week, as referenced by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Appendix J2. Actual water consumption will vary depending on the amount of laundry done.

columns.name: Integrated Water Factor (IWF)
columns.description: Integrated Water Factor (IWF) is a measure of water efficiency that considers gallons of water consumed per cubic foot of capacity. The lower the IWF, the more water efficient the clothes washer.

columns.name: Annual Energy Use (kWh/yr)
columns.description: This is the estimated annual energy use of the clothes washer under typical conditions, including the energy used by the washer and the energy needed to heat the water used by the clothes washer with an electric water heater. Unlike IMEF, the estimated annual energy use does not address how effective the washer is at removing moisture from the wash load and thus reducing the energy needed to fully dry a load in the clothes dryer. It is based on an annual usage of 295 loads per year, or around 6 loads per week, as referenced by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Appendix J2. Actual energy consumption will vary depending on various factors such as the amount of laundry done, the size of the loads, and the settings chosen.

columns.name: Integrated Modified Energy Factor (IMEF)
columns.description: IMEF is the energy performance metric for ENERGY STAR certified clothes washers. The higher the IMEF, the more energy efficient the clothes washer. The metric has units: ft3/kWh/cycle, and is calculated as the quotient of the capacity of the clothes container divided by the total clothes washer energy consumption per cycle, with such energy consumption expressed as the sum of the machine electrical energy consumption, the hot water energy consumption, the energy required for removal of the remaining moisture in the wash load, and the combined low-power mode energy consumption. For example, if a clothes washer has a tub volume of 4.0 cubic feet and a total clothes washer energy consumption per cycle of 10 kWh/cycle, then the IMEF is 2.5.

columns.name: Depth (inches)
columns.description: The depth of the product.

columns.name: Width (inches)
columns.description: The width of the product.

columns.name: Height (inches)
columns.description: The height of the product.

columns.name: Volume (cu. ft.)
columns.description: This is the tub capacity of the clothes washer in cubic feet, as measured by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Appendix J2.

columns.name: Additional Washer Features
columns.description: Features applicable to the clothes washer may include Steam Cycle, Anti-Wrinkle, Gentle Cycle, Delayed Start, Sanitize Option, Hand Wash Cycle, and Supplementary Wash System.

columns.name: Special Type
columns.description: This refers to whether the product is a combination all-in-one washer-dryer or laundry center.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Intended Market
columns.description: This refers to whether the product is intended to be sold in the residential market, the commercial/multi-family housing/coin laundry market, or both.

columns.name: Paired ENERGY STAR Clothes Dryer Available
columns.description: Indicates if the clothes washer is being marketed as a matched pair with an ENERGY STAR Clothes Dryer.

columns.name: Load Configuration
columns.description: Type refers to whether the product is a front-loading washer or a top-loading washer.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 8.0 and Version 8.1 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Clothes Washers that are effective as of February 5, 2018. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/clothes_washers/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Residential Refrigerators

columns.name: Connected Functionality Tested
columns.description: Refrigerator models listed with Connected Functionality must be tested using the ENERGY STAR Test Method to Validate Demand Response to verify the model’s Demand Response (DR) Functionality. Coolers are not required to be tested.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what you need in your home to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control, e.g. ‘WiFi’ or Zigbee.

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for residential refrigerators include R-600a and R-441A.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the type of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: Percent Less Energy Use than US Federal Standard
columns.description: ENERGY STAR certified refrigerators and coolers must generally exceed the U.S. federal standard by at least 10%. This column tells you by how much each model exceeds the standard.

columns.name: US Federal Standard (kWh/yr)
columns.description: The U.S. federal standard is the maximum energy consumption in kWh/year allowed by the U.S. Department of Energy for a refrigerator or cooler of this particular configuration and size.

columns.name: Communication Standard Application Layer
columns.description: Open Standard - Application Layer include SEP 1.x, SEP2.x, OpenADR, etc.

columns.name: Width (in)
columns.description: Measured from side to side.

columns.name: Counter Depth
columns.description: This refrigerator or cooler design is approximately 24 inches deep without the door, making them nearly flush with standard kitchen cabinets. With the doors, they are generally 27-29 inches deep.

columns.name: Ice Maker
columns.description: Included in some refrigerators for making ice cubes.

columns.name: Direct on-premises Open-standard Based Interconnection
columns.description: Connected appliances may or may not enable open-standards connectivity in the home. Consider the following illustrative examples: "Yes" An appliance that can directly communicate using Wi-Fi and OpenADR, or wired Ethernet and SEP 2.x; "No" An appliance that uses proprietary communications to/from the cloud, and is capable of supporting open-standards communications only in the cloud.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Annual Energy Use (kWh/yr)
columns.description: This number is the estimated annual energy use in kWh/year of the refrigerator or cooler under typical conditions. Your actual energy consumption will vary depending on how you use your refrigerator or cooler, such as frequency of opening and closing, surrounding room temperature, and how full the unit is kept. This figure is calculated according to U.S. Department of Energy test procedures.

columns.name: Adjusted Volume (ft3)
columns.description: This is a figure accounting for the fact that freezers require more energy than refrigerators per unit of actual volume, because they are colder. The adjusted volume is the sum of the fresh food compartment volume, the cooler compartment volume, and the product of an adjustment factor and the net freezer compartment volume in cubic feet. It is used to determine the federal energy conservation standards for refrigerators, freezers, and coolers. Adjusted Volume = Fresh Food Volume + Cooler Volume + (1.76 x Freezer Volume).

columns.name: Capacity (Total Volume) (ft3)
columns.description: The total interior volume of the refrigerator, including both the refrigerator and freezer compartments. Generally, the larger the refrigerator, the greater the energy consumption.

columns.name: Height (in)
columns.description: Measured from the floor to top of hinge.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Connected Functionality
columns.description: Indicates whether the model offers advanced controls and increased functionality such as sending notifications when the refrigerator or cooler door is not closed or allowing for a service center to remotely diagnose problems. Refrigerators or coolers with connected functionality are smart grid ready; if linked with your energy provider or an energy management system these products can dynamically adjust their energy use, potentially lowering your electric bill.

columns.name: Thru the Door Dispenser
columns.description: Allows access to ice and/or water through an exterior dispenser. Automatic icemakers and through the door dispensers offer convenience but they can increase energy use by 14-20%.

columns.name: Built-in
columns.description: Built-in refrigerators are designed to be installed totally encased by kitchen cabinets.

columns.name: Compact
columns.description: Designation for refrigerators, refrigerator-freezers, and coolers with a total volume of less than 7.75 cubic feet and 36 inches or less in height.

columns.name: Defrost Type
columns.description: This is the type of defrost function. Automatic, manual, and partial automatic are the standard defrost types.

columns.name: Product Class
columns.description: Classification of products by the type of energy used or by capacity or other performance-related features that affect efficiency. Different ENERGY STAR levels may apply to different product classes.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: This shows the configuration of the refrigerator or cooler.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 5.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Residential Refrigerators and Freezers that are effective as of September 15, 2014 or the Version 5.1 Program Requirements that are effective as of August 5, 2021. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/refrigerators/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Residential Clothes Dryers

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the type of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Paired ENERGY STAR Clothes Washer ENERGY STAR Model Identifier
columns.description: Paired clothes washer ENERGY STAR Model Identifier .

columns.name: Estimated Energy Test Cycle Time (min)
columns.description: The estimated energy test cycle time is the time taken to complete the test cycle necessary for ENERGY STAR certification. Products must complete the energy test cycle in less than 80 minutes in order to be certified as ENERGY STAR. The estimated test cycle time does not reflect actual consumer cycle times which vary as a result of load size, dampness, and composition.

columns.name: Estimated Annual Energy Use (kWh/yr)
columns.description: The estimated annual energy use is based on the Combined Energy Factor and an annual usage of 283 cycles per year, as referenced by the U.S. Department of Energy test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Subpart B, Appendix D2. Actual energy consumption will vary on your usage patterns, including how often your run the dryer, what cycle you select, and load size. NOTE: For gas dryers the estimated annual energy use is expressed in kilowatt hours to allow for comparison, and represents the equivalent multi-source energy use of the product (e.g., natural gas or propane typically expressed in British thermal units (Btu)).

columns.name: Heat Pump Technology
columns.description: Highly energy efficient heat pump technology typically heats, dehumidifies and circulates air in the dryer drum, so no vent is required. A Heat Pump model solely uses heat pump technology to dry clothes, while a Hybrid Heat Pump model uses a combination of heat pump technology and traditional electric resistance heating technology.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Communication Standard - Application Layer
columns.description: Open Standard - Application Layer include SEP 1.x, SEP2.x, OpenADR, etc.

columns.name: Height (inches)
columns.description: The height of the clothes dryer.

columns.name: Drum Capacity (cu-ft)
columns.description: This is the drum capacity of the clothes dryers in cubic feet as measured by the U.S. Department of Energy test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Subpart B, Appendix D2. Products with a drum capacity less than 4.4 cubic feet are considered compact.

columns.name: Special Type
columns.description: Indicates if the clothes dryer is part of a laundry center.

columns.name: Width (inches)
columns.description: The width of the clothes dryer.

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Heat pump dryers require refrigerants as well. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower GWP alternative refrigerant options for clothes dryers include R-290.

columns.name: Depth (inches)
columns.description: The depth of the clothes dryer.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Fuel Type
columns.description: This field is used to indicate the fuel type used to heat the air necessary to dry the clothes, either electric or gas.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Direct on-premises Open-standard Based Interconnection
columns.description: Connected appliances may or may not enable open-standards connectivity in the home. Consider the following illustrative examples: "Yes" An appliance that can directly communicate using Wi-Fi and OpenADR, or wired Ethernet and SEP 2.x; "No" An appliance that uses proprietary communications to/from the cloud, and is capable of supporting open-standards communications only in the cloud.

columns.name: Vented or Ventless
columns.description: Venting Configuration refers to how the clothes dryer exhausts heated air. Vented clothes dryers exhaust the evaporated moisture from the clothes dryer cabinet using a vent which typically leads to a building’s exterior. Ventless clothes dryers use a closed loop system with an internal condenser to remove the evaporated moisture from the heated air. Moisture is discharged from the cabinet and instead collected in a reservoir or drained through a tube.

columns.name: Additional Dryer Features
columns.description: Features applicable to the clothes dryer may include drum light, wrinkle prevention option, steam cycle, sanitization cycle, filter cleaning indicator, energy monitor/indicator, time remaining display, and supplementary drying system.

columns.name: Combined Energy Factor (CEF)
columns.description: CEF is the energy performance metric for ENERGY STAR clothes dryers; the higher the CEF the more efficient the clothes dryer. The metric has units lbs/kWh and is calculated by dividing the weight of the test load by the sum of the per cycle standby and off mode energy consumption and either the total per-cycle electricity dryer energy consumption or the total per-cycle gas dryer energy consumption expressed in kilowatt hours (kWh).

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what you need in your home to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control, e.g. ‘WiFi’ or Zigbee.

columns.name: Calculated Combined Energy Factor - Max Dryness Setting (lbs/kWh)
columns.description: The Calculated CEF – Max Dry is a measure of the dryer’s energy performance in a more energy consumptive setting, namely the normal cycle with the highest dryness setting as defined in the ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2017 criteria. The Calculated CEF – Max Dry should not be directly compared to the CEF field, which is tested using the medium dryness setting, and represents a rated rather than measured value. The CEF metric has units lbs/kWh and is calculated by dividing the weight of the test load by the sum of the per cycle standby and off mode energy consumption and either the total per-cycle electricity dryer energy consumption or the total per-cycle gas dryer energy consumption expressed in kilowatt hours (kWh).

columns.name: Energy Test Cycle Information
columns.description: The cycle selection and settings (temperature and dryness where separately selectable) as defined in the U.S. Department of Energy test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Subpart B, Appendix D2, Section 3.3, used for certification to ENERGY STAR.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Paired ENERGY STAR Clothes Washer Available
columns.description: Indicates if the clothes dryer is being marketed as a matched pair with an ENERGY STAR Clothes Washer.

columns.name: Connected
columns.description: Indicates whether the model offers advanced controls and increased functionality such as sending notifications when the dryer is done or allowing for a service center to remotely diagnose problems. Clothes dryers with connected functionality are smart grid ready; if linked with your energy provider or an energy management system these products can dynamically adjust their energy use, potentially lowering your electric bill.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

description: Certified dryers and combination all-in-one washer-dryers that meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 1.1 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Clothes Dryers that are effective as of January 1, 2015. Combination all-in-one washer-dryers also meet the Version 8.1 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Clothes Washers that are effective as of February 5, 2018. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at: https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/clothes_dr

columns.name: Refrigerant Type

columns.name: COP Rating
columns.description: A measure of efficiency in the heating mode that represents the ratio of total heating capacity to electrical energy input.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Energy Efficiency (EER)
columns.description: A measure of efficiency in the cooling mode that represents the ratio of total cooling capacity to electrical energy input.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: A geothermal heat pump uses the thermal energy of the ground or groundwater to provide residential space conditioning and/or domestic water heating. A geothermal heat pump model normally consists of one or more factory-made assemblies that include indoor conditioning and/or domestic water heat exchanger(s), compressors, and a ground-side heat exchanger. A geothermal heat pump model may provide space heating, space cooling, domestic water heating, or a combination of these functions and may also include the functions of liquid circulation, thermal storage, air circulation, air cleaning, dehumidifying or humidifying. A geothermal heat pump system generally consists of one or more geothermal heat pump models, the ground heat exchanger(s), the air and/or hydronic space conditioning distribution system(s), temperature controls, and thermal storage tanks.

columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Efficiency (AFUE)
columns.description: AFUE: The Annual Fuel Utilization Efficiency ("AFUE") measures the amount of fuel converted to space heat in proportion to the amount of fuel entering the boiler. This is commonly expressed as a percentage. CAafue: The Combined Appliance Annual Fuel Utilization Efficiency (“CAafue”) is the effective efficiency of the combined appliance in performing the function of space heating.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Ventilating Fans

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Lamp ESUID
columns.description: This column indicates the ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) number for the ENERGY STAR lamps that are shipped with the certified ceiling fan.
