The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Sound Level (sones)
columns.description: Residential bath and utility ventilating fans and range hoods must meet maximum allowable sound levels between 2-3 sones. There are no sound requirements for single or multi-port in-line fans.

columns.name: Energy Efficiency - Measured Outside the Fixture (lm/W)
columns.description: The quotient of the total luminous flux (lumens) emitted by the fixture over the total power input (Watts). It is expressed in lumens per watt (lm/W). For ENERGY STAR qualified fixtures this applies to light fixtures that are considered directional and are measured at the fixture level. This includes the following residential grade directional fixture types: line-voltage track lights, ceiling fan light kits, undercabinet and cove lighting, downlights, recessed, pendant or surface mounted and includes retrofit kits for existing recessed cans. This also includes the following LIMITED commercial fixture types: Accent lights such as line-voltage track lights, under cabinet task lights, portable desk task lights, downlights, recessed, pendant or surface-mounts BUT EXCLUDES typical commercial and industrial fixtures such as recessed troffers or linear forms (e.g. linear fluorescent pendants) typically used in office spaces.

columns.name: Notes
columns.description: Indicates information on certification relevant to the consumer (i.e. product variations, unique issues concerning the model’s listing, etc.).

columns.name: Airflow 1 (cfm)
columns.description: It is standard industry practice to round down airflow to the nearest 10 cfm when publishing this metric in product directories as the Airflow Certified Rating. For this reason, Airflow Certified Rating and As-Tested Airflow may occasionally differ on this list. In the case of a range hood, Airflow Certified Rating represents performance at working speed, while in the case of a bathroom or utility room fan, or an in-line ventilating fan, it represents maximum speed.

columns.name: Efficacy 1 (cfm/Watt)
columns.description: Efficacy is calculated by using airflow and fan motor electrical power values as tested per the requirements of this specification. Fan motor electrical usage is the only energy consumption considered for the fan efficacy calculation. Energy used for other fan auxiliaries (e.g., lights, sensors, heaters, timers, or night lights) is not included in the determination of fan efficacy.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Duct Size
columns.description: Duct size indicates circular diameter for circular ducts and depth by width for rectangular ducts.

columns.name: Bathroom and Utility Room Sound Level (sones) at 0.25 in. w.g.
columns.description: This measurement showcases the sound performance of a fan that is installed and operating under a heavier load than it is designed for.

columns.name: Bathroom and Utility Room Airflow at 0.25 in. w.g.
columns.description: This measurement showcases the airflow performance of a fan that is installed and operating under a heavier load than it is designed for.

columns.name: Total Light Output (lumens)
columns.description: The amount of light emitted by the light source or light fixture. Non directional fixtures (with removable light sources i.e. GU24 base bulbs) are measured at the light source and do not account for light lost from the fixture, while other fixtures meant to put light on a specific application (e.g. recessed downlights, under cabinet lights, accent lights, cove lights) are measured at the fixture level, only counting the light that escapes the fixture.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The type of ventilating fan.

columns.name: Lighting
columns.description: Indicates if the unit includes a light source.

columns.name: Power Factor
columns.description: Ratio of real power flowing to the lamp to the apparent power in the circuit. A lamp with a lower power factor will draw more current than a lamp with a higher power factor of the same wattage.

columns.name: Light Color Quality (CRI)
columns.description: Measure of the degree of color shift objects undergo when illuminated by the light source as compared with the color of those same objects when illuminated by a reference source of comparable color temperature.

columns.name: Efficacy 3 (cfm/Watt)
columns.description: Efficacy is calculated by using airflow and fan motor electrical power values as tested per the requirements of this specification. Fan motor electrical usage is the only energy consumption considered for the fan efficacy calculation. Energy used for other fan auxiliaries (e.g., lights, sensors, heaters, timers, or night lights) is not included in the determination of fan efficacy.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Airflow 3 (cfm)
columns.description: It is standard industry practice to round down airflow to the nearest 10 cfm when publishing this metric in product directories as the Airflow Certified Rating. For this reason, Airflow Certified Rating and As-Tested Airflow may occasionally differ on this list. In the case of a range hood, Airflow Certified Rating represents performance at working speed, while in the case of a bathroom or utility room fan, or an in-line ventilating fan, it represents maximum speed.

columns.name: Number of Speeds
columns.description: The number of speeds available for a ventilating fan. For fans with three or more speeds, only 3 is displayed.

columns.name: Lamp Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. This model number pertains only to the lamp.

columns.name: Light Source Life (Hours)
columns.description: The expected useful life of the light source. Measured differently depending on the technology of the light source. For example, LED lighting expected useful life is based on the estimated point at which only 70% of the original light output remains, while for older technologies, like fluorescent and halogen, estimated useful life is based on the measured median sample life.

columns.name: Total Input Power (Watts)
columns.description: Total power consumed by the luminaire while in use.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler.

columns.name: Airflow 2 (cfm)
columns.description: It is standard industry practice to round down airflow to the nearest 10 cfm when publishing this metric in product directories as the Airflow Certified Rating. For this reason, Airflow Certified Rating and As-Tested Airflow may occasionally differ on this list. In the case of a range hood, Airflow Certified Rating represents performance at working speed, while in the case of a bathroom or utility room fan, or an in-line ventilating fan, it represents maximum speed.

columns.name: Special Features (Dimming, Motion Sensing, etc.)
columns.description: Most fixtures are designed to operate on an on/off switch. However, some fixtures have been designed with features to perform in specialized applications, such as with dimmers, three-way, or photo cell controls. Special features and compatibility with controls should be clearly marked on the product packaging, cut sheet or web site for the product.

columns.name: Shipped with ENERGY STAR Lamp(s)
columns.description: For ventilating fans with lighting, this column indicates whether the ceiling fan is shipped with ENERGY STAR certified lamps.

columns.name: MERV of In-line Fan Filter
columns.description: Some in-line fans are shipped with a filter. The minimum efficiency reporting value (MERV) rating indicates the level of filtration. A higher MERV rating means a greater level of filtration.

columns.name: Alternate ENERGY STAR Lamps ESUIDs
columns.description: This column indicates alternate ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) number(s) for the ENERGY STAR lamps that are shipped with the certified ventilating fan.

columns.name: Lighting Technology
columns.description: The light source technology utilized by the fixture: compact fluorescent light (CFL), light emitting diode (LED), or halogen.

columns.name: Light Color Appearance (CCT)
columns.description: A description of the "color" of a light source measured on the Kelvin (K) scale. CFLs are available in a range of color temperatures. Lower values (2700-3000K) mean the white light has a warmer (red-yellow) tone, while higher kelvin (4100-6500K) mean the white light has a cooler (bluer) tone.

columns.name: Efficacy 2 (cfm/Watt)
columns.description: Efficacy is calculated by using airflow and fan motor electrical power values as tested per the requirements of this specification. Fan motor electrical usage is the only energy consumption considered for the fan efficacy calculation. Energy used for other fan auxiliaries (e.g., lights, sensors, heaters, timers, or night lights) is not included in the determination of fan efficacy.

columns.name: Date Qualified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 4.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Ventilating Fans that were effective as of October 1, 2015 or the Version 4.1 requirements that are effective as of July 24, 2018. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/heating_cooling/fans_ventilating/key_product_criteria .

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Imaging Equipment

columns.name: Page Format Size
columns.description: 1) Large Format: Products designed for A2 media and larger, including those designed to accommodate continuous-form media greater than or equal to 406 mm wide. Large-format products may also be capable of printing on standard-size or small-format media. 2) Standard Format: Products designed for standard-sized media (e.g., Letter, Legal, Ledger, A3, A4, B4), including those designed to accommodate continuous-form media between 210 mm and 406 mm wide. Standard-size products may also be capable of printing on small-format media. 3) Small Format: Products designed for media sizes smaller than those defined as Standard (e.g., A6, 4”x6”, microfilm), including those designed to accommodate continuous-form media less than 210 mm wide.

columns.name: Remanufactured Product
columns.description: Required for all submissions which contain remanufactured products. Indicate if the certified product or product family includes remanufactured products.

columns.name: Professional Imaging Product
columns.description: Indicates whether the product is a Professional Imaging Product.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: Eligible product type include: printer, scanner, copier, fax machine, multifunction device (MFD), digital duplicator, or mailing machine.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Professional Imaging Product Ready Mode Power (W)
columns.description: The power required by a Professional Imaging Product to maintain its Ready Mode.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Functional Adders
columns.description: A functional adder is a data and network interface or other feature that adds functionality to the marking engine of an imaging equipment product and provides a power allowance when qualifying products according to the OM method.

columns.name: Date Qualified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Print/Copy Time from Ready State (s)
columns.description: The time between job initiation and first sheet exiting the Imaging Equipment product, with the measurement taken after the product has been powered on and has indicated it is in Ready State.

columns.name: Power in Sleep (Watts)
columns.description: The power measured when a product enters Sleep Mode, either automatically after a period of inactivity, in response to user manual action, or in response to external electrical stimulus.

columns.name: Print Speed (ipm or mppm)
columns.description: The maximum claimed monochrome (black and white) speed in ipm (images per minute) and mppm (mail pieces per minute) when processing the given media.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: DFE Sleep Mode Power (Watts)
columns.description: The average power of the DFE recorded while the main Imaging Equipment product is in Sleep Mode, applicable to DFEs with network-capable Sleep Modes.

columns.name: Professional Imaging Product Production Energy (Wh/image)
columns.description: A performance metric that indicates the energy required by a  Professional Imaging Product to produce an image once in its Production Mode. A lower number translates to a higher efficiency.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: DFE Ready State Power (Watts)
columns.description: The average power of the DFE recorded while the main Imaging Equipment product is in Ready State.

columns.name: DFE Typical Electricity Consumption (TEC) (kWh/yr)
columns.description: Annualized TEC of the DFE.

columns.name: DFE Typical Electricity Consumption (TEC) (kWh/wk)
columns.description: The DFE Typical Electricity Consumption (TECDFE) metric assumes 9 hours in Ready State five days a week and the remainder in Sleep Mode. DFEs without a Sleep Mode are assumed to be in Ready State 168 hours per week.

columns.name: DFE Model Number
columns.description: The model number of the DFE. Again, the model number may be the same as or different from that of the Imaging Equipment product.

columns.name: Digital Front End (DFE) Manufacturer
columns.description:
A DFE is functionally-integrated server that hosts other computers and applications and acts as an interface to Imaging Equipment. A DFE provides greater functionality to the Imaging Equipment. A DFE offers three or more of the following advanced features: network connectivity in various environments; mailbox functionality; job queue management; machine management (e.g., waking the Imaging Equipment from a reduced power state); advanced graphic user-interface (UI); ability to initiate communication with other host servers and client computers (e.g., scanning to email, polling remote mailboxes for jobs); or ability to post-process pages (e.g., reformatting pages prior to printing). 

The manufacturer may be the same as or different from that of the Imaging Equipment product.

columns.name: Print/Copy Time from Previous Job (s)
columns.description: The time between job initiation and first sheet exiting the Imaging Equipment product with the measurement taken 15 minutes after the start of a previous job.

columns.name: Print/Copy Time from Sleep Mode (s)
columns.description: The time between job initiation and first sheet exiting the Imaging Equipment product, with the measurement taken after the product has been in Sleep Mode for 1 hour.

columns.name: Default Delay Time to Sleep (minutes)
columns.description: The time set by the manufacturer prior to shipping that determines when the product will enter a lower-power Mode (e.g., Sleep, Auto-off) following completion of its primary function.

columns.name: Power in Off (Watts)
columns.description: The power measured when the product has been manually or automatically switched off but is still plugged in and connected to the mains.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Typical Electricity Consumption (TEC) (kWh/wk)
columns.description: Typical electricity consumption (measured in kilowatt-hours) during normal operation over a specified period of time. Typical Electricity Consumption (TEC) is a method of testing and comparing the energy performance of certain imaging equipment products and focuses on the typical electricity consumed by a product while in normal operation during a representative period of time. The TEC metric is a proxy for a typical weekly electricity consumption. Please note: the TEC value should be used as a way to compare products' energy consumption values under a set duty cycle and operating conditions. Actual energy consumption will differ, based on how the product is used. TEC is not applicable to all imaging equipment products.

columns.name: Automatic Duplex Output Capable
columns.description:
Whether or not a copier, fax machine, MFD, or printer has the capability to produce images on both sides of an output sheet, without manual manipulation of output as an intermediate step. 

A product is considered to have automatic duplexing capability only if all accessories needed to produce duplex output are included with the product upon shipment.

columns.name: Color Capability
columns.description: Whether the product's output is monochrome (shades of black and white) or color.

columns.name: Marking Technology
columns.description: The marking technology of the product: direct thermal, dye sublimation, electro-photographic, impact, ink jet, high performance ink jet, solid ink, stencil, thermal transfer.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 3.0 and Version 3.1 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Imaging Equipment that are effective as of October 11, 2019 and the V3.2 Program Requirements that are effective as of November 18, 2021. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/office_equipment/imaging_equipment/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Furnaces

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Date Qualified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Can Integrate Hot Water Heating
columns.description: An indicator of whether the furnace is capable of providing hot-water heating.

columns.name: Fuel Type
columns.description: The type of fuel the furnace uses to operate.

columns.name: Furnace is ENERGY STAR Certified in
columns.description: This column indicates the climate region of the United States where the furnace qualifies as ENERGY STAR. For a listing of states in each climate region, please visit: http://www.energystar.gov/index.cfm?c=furnaces.pr_crit_furnaces

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: This list/dataset includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Capable of Two Way Communication?
columns.description: An indicator of whether the furnace is capable of two-way communication with any system controllers.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient Criteria
columns.description: Note: EPA suspended ENERGY STAR Most Efficient recognition for furnaces for 2022.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Efficiency (AFUE)
columns.description: The percentage of the heat in the incoming fuel which is converted to space heat instead of being lost.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 4.1 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Furnaces that are effective as of February 1, 2013. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/heating_cooling/furnaces/key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Residential Freezers

columns.name: US Federal Standard (kWh/yr)
columns.description: The U.S. federal standard is the maximum energy consumption in kWh/year allowed by the U.S. Department of Energy for a freezer of this particular configuration and size.

columns.name: Thru the Door Dispenser
columns.description: Indicates if the model has the through-the-door ice feature. It does not indicate whether or not the model has an ice maker. Please contact the manufacturer directly to determine if the model has an ice maker.

columns.name: Compact
columns.description: Designation for freezers with a total volume of less than 7.75 cubic feet and 36 inches or less in height.

columns.name: Defrost Type
columns.description: Freezers are available with either manual or automatic defrost. Manual defrost freezers use about half the energy of automatic defrost models, but must be defrosted periodically to achieve the energy savings. Don’t allow frost to build up more than one quarter of an inch.

columns.name: Adjusted Volume (ft3)
columns.description: This is a figure accounting for the fact that freezers require more energy than refrigerators per unit of actual volume, because they are colder. The adjusted volume is the sum of the fresh food compartment volume and the product of an adjustment factor and the net freezer compartment volume in cubic feet. It's used to determine the federal energy conservation standards for refrigerators and freezers. Refrigerator Adjusted Volume = Fresh Volume + (1.63 x Freezer Volume). Freezer Adjusted Volume = 1.73 x Freezer Volume.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Product Class
columns.description: Classification of products by the type of energy used or by capacity or other performance-related features that affect efficiency. Different ENERGY STAR levels may apply to different product classes.

columns.name: Ice Maker
columns.description: Included in some refrigerators for making ice cubes.

columns.name: Connected Functionality
columns.description: Indicates whether the model offers advanced controls and increased functionality such as sending notifications when the freezer door is not closed or allowing for a service center to remotely diagnose problems. Freezers with connected functionality are smart grid ready; if linked with your energy provider or an energy management system these products can dynamically adjust their energy use, potentially lowering your electric bill.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for residential freezers include R-600a and R-441A.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: Capacity (Total Volume) (ft3)
columns.description: The total interior volume of the freezer. Generally, the larger the freezer, the greater the energy consumption.

columns.name: Built-in
columns.description: Built-in freezers are designed to be installed totally encased by kitchen cabinets.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the type of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: Percent Less Energy Use than US Federal Standard
columns.description: ENERGY STAR certified residential freezers must generally exceed the U.S. federal standard by at least 10%. This column tells you by how much each model exceeds the standard.

columns.name: Annual Energy Use (kWh/yr)
columns.description: This number is the estimated annual energy use in kilowatt hours of the freezer under typical conditions. Your actual energy consumption will vary depending on how you use your freezer, such as frequency of opening and closing, surrounding room temperature, and how full the unit is kept. This figure is calculated according to U.S. Department of Energy test procedures.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: Freezers are available in both chest and upright configurations. An upright freezer has a front-mounted door like a refrigerator and shelves that allow for easy organization. A chest freezer typically requires more floor space, but they are also more energy efficient and since the door opens from the top it will allow less cold air to escape.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 5.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Residential Refrigerators and Freezers that are effective as of September 15, 2014. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/refrigerators/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Model Index

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria for 2025. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 criteria recognizes the most efficient products in select product categories among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information on product categories included in ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 and more, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

description: This data set contains a simplified list of all currently certified ENERGY STAR models with basic model information collected across all product categories including ENERGY STAR Unique IDs, ENERGY STAR partners, model names and numbers, and brand names. Learn more about ENERGY STAR products at www.energystar.gov/products. A full list of ENERGY STAR specifications can be found at www.energystar.gov/specifications.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Commercial Clothes Washers

columns.name: Water Factor (WF)
columns.description: WF is the water performance metric for ENERGY STAR qualified clothes washers. The lower the WF, the more efficient the clothes washer. The metric has units: gallons/cycle/ft3, and is calculated as the quotient of total weighted per-cycle water consumption divided by the capacity of the clothes washer. For example, if a clothes washer has a total weighted per-cycle water consumption of 20 gallons and a tub volume of 4.0 cubic feet, then the water factor is 5.0.

columns.name: Load Configuration
columns.description: Type refers to whether the product is a front-loading washer or a top-loading washer.

columns.name: US Federal Standard (WF)
columns.description: The mandatory water efficiency standards for clothes washers, established by DOE rulemaking activities and federal laws.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: A Universal Product Code (UPC) is a specific type of barcode for tracking items sold in stores.

columns.name: Additional Washer Features
columns.description: Features applicable to the clothes washer may include Steam Cycle, Anti-Wrinkle, Gentle Cycle, Delayed Start, Sanitize Option, Hand Wash Cycle, and Supplementary Wash System.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Intended Market
columns.description: This refers to whether the product is intended to be sold in the residential market, the commercial/multi-family housing/coin laundry market, or both.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Integrated Water Factor (IWF)
columns.description: IWF is the water performance metric for ENERGY STAR certified clothes washers. The lower the IWF, the more efficient the clothes washer. The metric has units: gallons/cycle/ft3, and is calculated as the quotient of total weighted per-cycle water consumption divided by the capacity of the clothes washer. For example, if a clothes washer has a total weighted per-cycle water consumption of 20 gallons and a tub volume of 4.0 cubic feet, then the IWF is 5.0.

columns.name: Volume (cu. ft.)
columns.description: This is the tub capacity of the clothes washer in cubic feet, as measured by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Appendix J2.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Annual Energy Use (kWh/yr)
columns.description: This is the estimated annual energy use of the clothes washer under typical conditions, including the energy used by the washer and the energy needed to heat the water used by the clothes washer with an electric water heater. Unlike MEF, the estimated annual energy use does not address how effective the washer is at removing moisture from the wash load and thus reducing the energy needed to fully dry a load in the clothes dryer. It is based on an annual usage of 1,074 loads per year for multi-family and 1,483 loads per year for laundromat applications, or around 21 and 29 loads per week respectively, as referenced by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Appendix J2. Actual energy consumption will vary depending on various factors such as the amount of laundry done, the size of the loads, and the settings chosen.

columns.name: Modified Energy Factor (MEF)
columns.description: MEF is the energy performance metric for ENERGY STAR certified commercial clothes washers. The higher the MEF, the more energy efficient the clothes washer. The metric has units: ft3/kWh/cycle, and is calculated as the quotient of the capacity of the clothes container divided by the total clothes washer energy consumption per cycle, with such energy consumption expressed as the sum of the machine electrical energy consumption, the hot water energy consumption, and the energy required for removal of the remaining moisture in the wash load. For example, if a clothes washer has a tub volume of 4.0 cubic feet and a total clothes washer energy consumption per cycle of 10 kWh/cycle, then the MEF is 2.5.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: US Federal Standard (IWF)
columns.description: The mandatory water efficiency standards for clothes washers, established by DOE rule-making activities and federal laws.

columns.name: US Federal Standard (MEF)
columns.description: The mandatory energy efficiency standards for clothes washers, established by DOE rulemaking activities and federal laws.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 8.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Clothes Washers that are effective as of February 5, 2018. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/clothes_washers/key_product_criteria.

The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

description: A list of all UPC codes and corresponding model numbers provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models.
