name: ENERGY STAR Certified Ceiling Fans

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Light Sources Per Light Kit
columns.description: The number of light sources included in each light kit.

columns.name: Ceiling Fan Light Kit Warranty (yrs)
columns.description: Certified ceiling fan light kits with replaceable drivers shall provide a warranty of at least 3 years. Certified ceiling fan light kits with non-replaceable drivers shall provide a warranty of at least 5 years.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified certified products.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: A standard ceiling fan is a low-speed small-diameter ceiling fan that hangs greater than 10 inches from the ceiling. A low-mount HSSD ceiling fan is a high-speed small-diameter ceiling fan with a blade thickness greater than 1/8 inch that operates within safe blade tip speed limits. A hugger ceiling fan is a low-speed small-diameter ceiling fan that hangs less than or equal to 10 inches from the ceiling.

columns.name: Ceiling Fan Efficiency (CFM/W)
columns.description: The ratio of the total airflow to the total power consumption, in units of cubic feet per minute per watt (CFM/W). For standard and hugger ceiling fans, this is a weighted efficiency based on the ceiling fan performance in low speed, high speed, and standby mode. For low-mount high-speed small-diameter ceiling fans, this is a weighted efficiency based on the ceiling fan performance in high speed and standby mode.

columns.name: Ceiling Fan Features
columns.description: Additional sensors that may be built into a ceiling fan to provide added convenience and to increase energy savings.

columns.name: Shipped with ENERGY STAR Lamp(s)
columns.description: For ceiling fans with a light kit, this column indicates whether the ceiling fan is shipped with ENERGY STAR certified lamps

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Lamp ESUID
columns.description: This column indicates the ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) number for the ENERGY STAR lamps that are shipped with the certified ceiling fan.

columns.name: Lamp Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. This model number pertains only to the lamp.

columns.name: Light Source Technology
columns.description: The light source technology utilized by the fixture: compact fluorescent light (CFL) or light emitting diode (LED).

columns.name: Light Source Connection/Base Type
columns.description: Light sources can be fully integrated into a light kit or removable by a consumer by custom connectors, wire nuts, pronged plugs like GU10, GU24 or sockets e.g. E26 known as the Edison base.

columns.name: Light Kit Dimmability
columns.description: This column indicates whether the light kit is dimmable or not.

columns.name: Special Lighting Features (Dimming, Motion Sensing, etc.)
columns.description: Most fixtures are designed to operate on an on/off switch. However, some fixtures have been designed with features to perform in specialized applications, such as with dimmers, three-way, or photo cell controls. Special features and compatibility with controls should be clearly marked on the product packaging, cut sheet or web site for the product.

columns.name: Connected Functionality
columns.description: Indicates whether the model offers advanced controls and increased functionality such as the ability to turn off the unit remotely when not at home and feedback on the energy use of the product. Connected functionality is optional and ceiling fans may be certified without it.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what you need in your home to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control (e.g., Wi-Fi or ZigBee).

columns.name: Notes
columns.description: Indicates information on certification relevant to the consumer (i.e. product variations, unique issues concerning the model’s listing, etc.).

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: This list/dataset includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Indoor/Outdoor
columns.description: This column indicates whether the ceiling fan is intended for indoor use only or can be used outdoors.

columns.name: Alternate ENERGY STAR Lamps ESUIDs
columns.description: This column indicates alternate ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) number(s) for the ENERGY STAR lamps that are shipped with the certified ceiling fan.

columns.name: Lighting Efficiency – Measured at the Source (lm/W)
columns.description: Proposed field definition: The quotient of the total luminous flux (lumens) emitted by the light source over the total power input (Watts). It is expressed in lumens per watt (lm/W).

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Power Factor of Light Kit
columns.description: Ratio of real power flowing to the lamp to the apparent power in the circuit. A lamp with a lower power factor will draw more current than a lamp with a higher power factor of the same wattage.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the certified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Blade Span (Diameter) (in.)
columns.description: The measurement of the largest swept circle by any part of the fan blade assembly. In other words, the diameter of the ceiling fan.

columns.name: Fan Power Consumption-Low Speed (W)
columns.description: Power consumed by the fan when operating at low speed, measured in Watts.

columns.name: Fan Power Consumption-High Speed (W)
columns.description: Power consumed by the fan when operating at high speed, measured in Watts.

columns.name: Fan Power Consumption-Standby (W)
columns.description: Power consumed by the fan when operating in standby mode, measured in Watts.

columns.name: Ceiling Fan Components Warranty (Years)
columns.description: Certified ceiling fans shall provide a warranty of at least 3 years for all non-lighting components.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Lamp Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR certified products.

columns.name: Total Light Output (lumens)
columns.description: The amount of light emitted by the light source or light fixture. Fixtures (with removable light sources  are measured at the light source and do not account for light lost from the fixture, while other fixtures, where the light source cannot be removed, are measured at the fixture level, only counting the light that escapes the fixture. Ceiling fan light kits shipped with ENERGY STAR Lamps do not have to meet minimum light output requirements.

columns.name: Total Lighting Input Power (Watts)
columns.description: Total power consumed by the light kit (luminaire) while in use.

columns.name: Lighting Efficiency – Measured outside the Fixture (lm/W)
columns.description: Proposed field definition: The quotient of the total luminous flux (lumens) emitted by the fixture over the total power input (Watts). It is expressed in lumens per watt (lm/W).

columns.name: Light Color Appearance (CCT)
columns.description: A description of the "color" of a light source measured on the Kelvin (K) scale. CFLs are available in a range of color temperatures. Lower values (2700-3000K) mean the white light has a warmer (red-yellow) tone, while higher kelvin (4100-6500K) mean the white light has a cooler (bluer) tone.

columns.name: Light Color Quality (CRI)
columns.description: The Color Rendering Index (CRI) of a light source represents the degree of color shift objects undergo when illuminated by the light source as compared with the color of those same objects when illuminated by a reference source of comparable color temperature. The ENERGY STAR specifications require a CRI of at least 80. Incandescent lighting has a CRI of 100. Early fluorescent lighting had CRIs in the 60s, resulting in unpleasant appearance of skin tones.

columns.name: Light Source Rated Life (Hours)
columns.description: The expected useful life of the light source. Measured differently depending on the technology of the light source. For example, LED lighting expected useful life is based on the estimated point at which only 70% of the original light output remains, while for older technologies, like fluorescent, estimated useful life is based on the measured median sample life.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 4.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Ceiling Fans that are effective as of June 15, 2018. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/ceiling_fans/ceiling_fans_key_product_criteria .

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Displays

columns.name: Reported Contrast Ratio at -85 Degrees (Left) Horizontal Viewing Angle
columns.description: Display contrast ratio at -85 degrees from the perpendicular, expressed as a integer relative to 1 (e.g., 1000 means 1000:1).

columns.name: Display Contrast Ratio  at 0 deg. Horiz. Viewing Angle
columns.description: Display contrast ratio at 0 degrees from the perpendicular, expressed as a integer relative to 1 (e.g., 1000 means 1000:1).

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: The 2 types of Displays eligible for ENERGY STAR certification are: Computer Monitors and Signage Displays.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Maximum Luminance (candelas per square meter)
columns.description: The photometric measure of the luminous intensity per unit area of light travelling in a given direction, expressed in candelas per square meter (cd/m^2). Luminance refers to the brightness settings of a display.

columns.name: Signal or Data Interfaces
columns.description: Available methods of delivering content to the display.

columns.name: Screen Area (square inches)
columns.description: The visible area of the Display that produces images.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Enhanced-Performance Display?
columns.description: A computer monitor that has all of the following features and functionalities: (a) A contrast ratio of at least 60:1 measured at a horizontal viewing angle of at least 85° from the perpendicular on a flat screen or 83° on a curved screen, with or without a screen cover glass; (b) A native resolution greater than or equal to 2.3 megapixels (MP); and, (c) A color gamut 32.9% of CIE LUV.

columns.name: Meets Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: USB-C with Power Delivery Supported
columns.description: Does the model tested include a USB Type C port with Power Delivery capability?

columns.name: Maximum Sleep Mode Power Limit for Signage Certification (watts)
columns.description: The maximum Sleep Mode power requirement for a Signage Display model with the particular features to qualify for ENERGY STAR. The Power Consumption in Sleep Mode cannot exceed this value. Computer Monitors must meet Total Energy Consumption requirements.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Maximum Tiled Configuration
columns.description: The number of Signage Display panels in the Maximum Tiled Configuration.

columns.name: Maximum Power Delivery (W)
columns.description: The rated or reported maximum power which the model can deliver via USB-C connection (W).

columns.name: Reported Contrast Ratio at +85 Degrees (Right) Horizontal Viewing Angle
columns.description: Display contrast ratio at +85 degrees from the perpendicular, expressed as a integer relative to 1 (e.g., 1000 means 1000:1).

columns.name: On Mode Power at 300 Lux (watts)
columns.description: For ABC-enabled displays, this is the on-mode power drawn by the display in a room with bright ambient light.

columns.name: Monitor Total Energy Consumption at 115 Volts (kWh/yr)
columns.description: This calculation (for Computer Monitors only) assumes a daily duty cycle of 35% in On Mode and 65% hours in Sleep Mode over the course of a year. Signage Displays must meet On Mode, Sleep Mode, and Off Mode power requirements.

columns.name: Maximum Total Energy Consumption Limit for Monitor Certification (kWh/yr)
columns.description: The maximum Total Energy Consumption requirement for a Computer Monitor model with the given Screen Area and Resolution to qualify for ENERGY STAR. The Total Energy Consumption cannot exceed this value. Signage Displays must meet On Mode, Sleep Mode, and Off Mode power requirements.

columns.name: Color Gamut
columns.description: The range of color supported within the CIE LUV 1976 u' v' color space and calculated per Section 5.18 Gamut Area of the Information Display Measurements Standard Version 1.03.

columns.name: Color Spaces Supported
columns.description: A color space is a defined range of colors. Well known color spaces include sRGB, AdobeRGB and DCI-P3. Color spaces listed here are fully supported (100% of the defined range) by the Display.

columns.name: Tiled Display System
columns.description: Is the Signage Display model tested as a Tiled Display System?

columns.name: High Dynamic Range (HDR)
columns.description: The HDR performance level for models that meet the VESA DisplayHDR CTS v1.0 Section 5.1 criteria. The model must meet the HDR performance level for all three white luminance criteria, including the 10% Center Patch Minimum Requirement (cd/m2), Full-screen Flash Minimum Requirement (cd/m2), and Full-screen Long-duration Minimum Requirement (cd/m2). N/A if the model does not meet the VESA DisplayHDR criteria.

columns.name: Sleep Mode Power (watts)
columns.description: The power mode the product enters after receiving a signal from a connected device or an internal stimulus. The product may also enter this mode by virtue of a signal produced by user input. The product must wake on receiving a signal from a connected device, a network, a remote control, and/or an internal stimulus. While the product is in this mode, it is not producing a visible picture, with the possible exception of user-oriented or protective functions such as product information or status displays, or sensor-based functions.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Panel Type
columns.description: The most common screen types include TN LCD, IPS LCD, VA LCD, OLED, and Plasma.

columns.name: Native Resolution (pixels)
columns.description: The fixed physical pixel count of the display.

columns.name: Total Native Resolution (megapixels)
columns.description: The fixed physical pixel count of the display.

columns.name: Screen Size (inches)
columns.description: The diagonal screen size in inches.

columns.name: Model Features
columns.description: Additional features and networking options available on the display.

columns.name: Features Enabled in Default On Mode
columns.description: Features enabled in the default tested On Mode.

columns.name: Features Enabled in Default Sleep Mode
columns.description: Features enabled in the default tested Sleep Mode.

columns.name: Power Source
columns.description: Available methods of delivering power to the display.

columns.name: On Mode Power (watts)
columns.description: The power mode in which the product has been activated, and is providing one or more of its principal functions. The common terms "active," "in-use," and "normal operation" also describe this mode. The power in this mode is typically greater than the power in Sleep Mode and Off Mode.

columns.name: Maximum On Mode Power Limit for Signage Certification (watts)
columns.description: The maximum On Mode power requirement for a Signage Display model with the given Screen Area and Maximum Reported Luminance to qualify for ENERGY STAR. The Power Consumption in On Mode cannot exceed this value. Computer Monitors must meet Total Energy Consumption requirements.

columns.name: Off Mode Power (watts)
columns.description: The power mode in which the product is connected to a power source, and is not providing any On Mode or Sleep Mode functions. This mode may persist for an indefinite time. The product may only exit this mode by direct user actuation of a power switch or control. Some products may not have this mode.

columns.name: On Mode Power at 12 Lux at 115 Volts (W)
columns.description: For ABC-enabled displays, this is the on-mode power consumed by the display in a room with low ambient light.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 8.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Displays that came into effect on January 28, 2020. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at: https://www.energystar.gov/products/office_equipment/displays/displays_key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Commercial Fryers

columns.name: Energy Use (Idle Energy Rate) (Btu/hr)
columns.description: The average rate of energy consumed in Btu/h by the fryer while “holding” or “idling” the frying medium at the thermostat(s) set point.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Cooking Energy Efficiency (%)
columns.description: The quantity of energy input to the food product (i.e., french fries) during the cooking process; expressed as a percentage of the quantity of energy input to the fryer during the heavy-, medium-, and light-load tests.

columns.name: Fuel Type
columns.description: The type of fuel (e.g., gas, electric).

columns.name: Installation
columns.description: The type of product used for installation (Drop-In, Countertop, Floor)

columns.name: Date Qualified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Shortening Capacity (lbs)
columns.description: The total amount of shortening the fry can hold in pounds.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. This column includes alternative means to identify the model/model family (e.g., additional model names, additional model numbers, retailer SKUs, product descriptions).

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The type of fryer (e.g., split vat, standard vat, large vat).

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Width (in.)
columns.description: For a standard fryer, the distance between the inner side walls of the frypot. For split vat fryers, the dimensions for twice the width of one side of the frypot.

columns.name: Energy Use (Idle Energy Rate) (Watts)
columns.description: The average rate of energy consumed in watts by the fryer while “holding” or “idling” the frying medium at the thermostat(s) set point.

columns.name: Depth (in.)
columns.description: The internal vertical distance taken from the bottom of the vat to the oil capacity fill line.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 3.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Commercial Fryers that are effective as of October 1, 2016. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/commercial_food_service_equipment/commercial_fryers/key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Telephones

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Energy Efficient Ethernet - Fast
columns.description: A technology which enables reduced power consumption of Fast Ethernet interfaces during times of low data throughput. Specified by IEEE 802.3az.

columns.name: Low Power Wireless Technologies
columns.description: Low power wireless technologies supported by the model.

columns.name: Additional Handset Telephones Supported
columns.description: The number of additional handsets supported by the telephone.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. This column includes alternative means to identify the model/model family (e.g., additional model names, additional model numbers, retailer SKUs, product descriptions).

columns.name: Display Resolution (pixels)
columns.description: The native resolution of the telephone's display.

columns.name: External Proxy Capability
columns.description: The ability of a telephone to maintain network presence on behalf of an End Point Device.

columns.name: External Power Supply
columns.description: A component contained in a separate physical enclosure external to the telephone casing, designed to convert line voltage ac input from the mains to lower dc voltage(s) in order to provide power to the telephone.

columns.name: Fast Ethernet Ports
columns.description: The number of Fast (10/100) Ethernet ports present in the model.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Qualified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Gigabit Ethernet Ports
columns.description: The number of Gigabit (10/100/1000) Ethernet ports present in the model.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: The sound transmission mechanism of the telephone, either analog, Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP), or a hybrid of analog and VoIP.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Power Over Ethernet Class
columns.description: The class of Power Over Ethernet indicating the range of operating power of the device. PoE is a technology which enables the transfer of electrical power, along with data, to network end point devices through an Ethernet cable.

columns.name: Physical Data Ports
columns.description: Physical data ports on the model.

columns.name: Frequency
columns.description: Frequency ranges are used in cordless telephones primarily to allow for multiple devices to utilize the same ranges and to permit frequency-hopping by the phones. The term “frequency” refers to the rate at which an electromagnetic wave oscillates.

columns.name: Configuration
columns.description: The physical configuration of the telephone including: Cordless Telephone, a telephone with a base station and a handset; Corded Telephone, a telephone with a permanent physical connection between the handset and the network; Conference Telephone, a telephone without a handset that utilizes a speakerphone for all communications and is primarily used for conference calls; Additional Handset, a telephone designed for use with a multi-handset telephone system; or a Wireless (Wi-Fi) Telephone, a telephone that connects via Wi-Fi.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Energy Efficient Ethernet - Gigabit
columns.description: A technology which enables reduced power consumption of Gigabit Ethernet interfaces during times of low data throughput. Specified by IEEE 802.3az.

columns.name: Standby Power (W)
columns.description: Mode with the telephone is connected to a power source and a telephone line or other physical or wireless network connection and is capable of receiving a call. The telephone is not receiving or transmitting sound, and the handset is "on the hook" and the speakerphone is not engaged.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 3.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Telephony (cordless telephones and VoIP telephones) that are effective as of October 1, 2014. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/electronics/cordless_phones/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Laboratory Grade Refrigerators and Freezers

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: The product type of the product as defined in the specification.

columns.name: Total Number of Doors
columns.description: The total number of doors the product contains.

columns.name: Width
columns.description: Measurement from side to side.

columns.name: Daily Energy Consumption for Ultra-Low Temp Freezer (kWh/day/cu. ft.)
columns.description: Value is the average of daily energy consumption at -70F and -80F as calculated according to equation 1 in the test method. The daily energy consumption of the product normalized for volume for ULT products.

columns.name: Lab Grade Refrigerator or Freezer Energy Consumption (kWh/day)
columns.description: The total energy consumption measured during the 24-hour test period.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Depth (in)
columns.description: Distance from the top or surface to its bottom.

columns.name: Height
columns.description: Measurement from base to top.

columns.name: Installed Accessories
columns.description: Any additional accessories installed in the product under test in addition to internal ports, access holes, and drawer/shelf configuration.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. This column includes alternative means to identify the model/model family (e.g., additional model names, additional model numbers, retailer SKUs, product descriptions).

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Product Form Factor
columns.description: The orientation of the product, either upright or chest.

columns.name: Number of glass doors
columns.description: The number of doors the product contains in which 75% or greater of the front surface of the door is glass.

columns.name: Door Opening Orientation
columns.description: The direction and type of door opening of the product.

columns.name: Number of Internal Ports and/or Access Holes
columns.description: The number of internal ports and/or access holes present in the product.

columns.name: Number of Drawers or Shelves
columns.description: The number of drawers or shelves present in the product.

columns.name: Defrost Type
columns.description: The defrost type employed by the product.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: The refrigerant type used in the product.

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for lab grade refrigerators and freezers include R-744 (CO₂), R-290, R-1150, and R-170.

columns.name: Total Volume (cu. ft.)
columns.description: The total interior volume of the product.

columns.name: Average Test Cabinet  (ºC)
columns.description: The average of the cabinet temperature measured during the 24-hour test period.

columns.name: Peak Temperature Variance (ºC)
columns.description: Peak temperature variance measured across all temperature measurement devices.

columns.name: Steady State Energy Consumption (kWh/day)
columns.description: The total energy consumption measured during the steady state portion of the test period, which starts one hour after the final door opening occurs.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Number of solid doors
columns.description: The number of doors the product contains in which less than 75% of the front surface of the door is glass.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Maximum Daily Energy Allowance (kWh/day)
columns.description: The maximum daily energy allowance of the product.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 1.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Laboratory Grade Refrigerators and Freezers that are effective as of December 21, 2016 and the Version 2.0 Program Requirements that are effective as of June 30, 2025. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/other/laboratory_grade_refrigerators_and_freezers

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Commercial Griddles

columns.name: Date Qualified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: Designates whether the model is a single-sided or double-sided commercial griddle. A double-sided griddle has hinged upper griddle plates (platens) that swing down over the food, thereby cooking the food from both sides at once.

columns.name: Fuel Type
columns.description: Designates the fuel type used by the commercial griddle. Qualified commercial griddle models are either gas or electric.

columns.name: Tested Configuration
columns.description: This column designates whether the model was tested in the single-sided or double-sided configuration.

columns.name: Griddle Plate Type Description
columns.description: This field provides additional details as to the type of griddle plate used (e.g., polished steel or chrome ½-inch grooved plate).

columns.name: Width (ft.)
columns.description: Designates the width of the griddle in ft. For qualification of a product family, the 3 ft. model within the product family can serve as the representative model. (If product family includes units smaller than 3 ft.; however, each additional unit shall be tested and qualified separately from its product family.)

columns.name: Depth (ft.)
columns.description: Designates the depth of the griddle in ft.

columns.name: Cooking Surface (sq. ft.)
columns.description: Represents the surface area of the commercial griddle (width multiplied by the depth).

columns.name: Cooking Energy Efficiency (%)
columns.description: The ratio of energy absorbed by the food product to the total energy supplied to the griddle during cooking.

columns.name: Production Capacity (lbs/hr)
columns.description: The maximum production rate of the griddle while cooking a specified food product in accordance with the heavy-load cooking-energy efficiency test, expressed in pounds per hour. The production rate is the average rate at which the griddle brings a specified food product to a specified final temperature.

columns.name: Energy Use (Normalized Idle Energy) (Btu/h/sq. ft.)
columns.description: For gas griddles, this field expresses the rate of  griddle energy consumption while it is maintaining or holding at a stabilized operating condition or temperature. The idle rate is normalized based on the area of the (bottom) cooking surface.

columns.name: Energy Use (Normalized Idle Energy) (Watts/sq. ft.)
columns.description: For electric griddles, this field expresses the rate of  griddle energy consumption while it is maintaining or holding at a stabilized operating condition or temperature. The idle rate is normalized based on the area of the (bottom) cooking surface.

columns.name: Top Platen Type
columns.description: This field demonstrates whether the product has integrated or add-on top platens. This impacts the testing of the models as single- or double-sided.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 1.2 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Commercial Griddles that are effective as of May 8, 2009 or January 1, 2011. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/commercial_food_service_equipment/commercial_griddles/key_products_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Commercial Ice Machines

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Condenser Unit Model Number (if applicable)
columns.description: If the product type is RCU, this field provides the model number of the remote condenser unit that is to accompany the ice-making mechanism (i.e., what condenser the unit was tested with and qualifies for ENERGY STAR under).

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what is needed to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control, e.g. ‘WiFi’ or Zigbee.

columns.name: Connected Functionality
columns.description: Indicates whether the model offers advanced controls and increased functionality such as load shifting, ice level sensor and Demand Response exit points. Commercial ice machines with connected functionality are smart grid ready; if linked with your energy provider or an energy management system these products can dynamically adjust their energy use, potentially lowering electric bills.

columns.name: Potable Water Use (gal/100 lbs ice)
columns.description: The amount of potable water used in making ice, which is equal to the sum of the ice harvested, Dump or Purge Water, and the Harvest Water expressed in gal/100 lb [L/45.0 kg] of ice, stated in multiples of 0.1. Alternatively, the amount of water entering the ice maker per cycle can be measured.

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for commercial ice machines include R-290, R-450A, and R-513A.

columns.name: Ice Type
columns.description: The type of ice the ice maker produces. Options include batch-type (which include cube type ice and other batch technologies) and continuous-type (which include flake and nugget ice types).

columns.name: Equipment Type
columns.description: The type of equipment category the ice maker falls into. The three categories include Ice Making Head (IMH), Remote Condensing Unit (RCU) with remote compressor, Remote Condensing Unit (RCU) without remote compressor, and Self-Contained Unit (SCU).

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Ice Hardness Factor
columns.description: The net cooling effect of ice harvested (Btu/lb) divided by 144 (Btu/lb), expressed as a percent. The Ice Hardness Factor for batch type machines is assumed to be 100%, but must be tested and reported for continuous type machine certification. (For certain models this may exceed 100%.)

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Energy Use (kWh/100 lbs ice)
columns.description: The total energy input rate, stated in kWh/100 lb [kWh/45.0 kg] of ice. For continuous type commercial ice makers this reflects the measured energy consumption per 100 lbs ice, multiplied by the ice hardness adjustment factor. (For batch type commercial ice makers the ice hardness is assumed to be 100%, therefore no adjustment is needed.) For RCU (with remote compressor), total energy consumed shall include the energy use of the ice-making mechanism, the compressor and the remote condensing unit.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the refrigerant type used.

columns.name: Capabilities Summary
columns.description: This summarizes the connected functionality capabilities if the model has connected functionality.

columns.name: Comminucation Standard Application Layer
columns.description: Open Standard - Application Layer include SEP 1.x, SEP2.x, OpenADR, etc.

columns.name: Direct on-premises Open-standard Based Interconnection
columns.description: Connected products may or may not enable open-standards connectivity. Consider the following illustrative examples: "Yes" A product that can directly communicate using Wi-Fi and OpenADR, or wired Ethernet and SEP 2.x; "No" A product that uses proprietary communications to/from the cloud, and is capable of supporting open-standards communications only in the cloud.

columns.name: Harvest Rate (lbs ice/day)
columns.description: The gross weight of ice harvested, stated in lb/24 h [kg/24 h], stated in multiples of 1.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 3.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Automatic Commercial Ice Makers that are effective as of January 28, 2018 . A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria is available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/commercial_food_service_equipment/commercial_ice_makers/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Enterprise Servers

columns.name: Low Power I/O States End-User Enabling Required
columns.description: If low power I/O states require the end user to enable them.

columns.name: Processor or Core Reduced Power States Enabled on Shipment
columns.description: If processor or core reduced power states are enabled in hardware upon shipment.

columns.name: Storage Power Management Enabled in Hardware on Shipment
columns.description: If storage power management is enabled in hardware upon shipment.

columns.name: Other Power Saving Feature 3: End-User Enabling Required
columns.description: If Other Power Saving Feature 3 requires the end user to enable it.

columns.name: Other Power Saving Feature 3: Description
columns.description: If Other Power Saving Feature 3 is available, a description is provided here.

columns.name: Power Saving Features Available
columns.description: Types of power saving features available include dynamic voltage and frequency of scaling of processor(s), processor or core reduced power states, power capping, variable speed fan control based on power or thermal readings, low power memory states, and lower power I/O states.

columns.name: APA #2 Model Name
columns.description: An Auxiliary Processing Accelerator (APA) are computing expansion add-in cards installed in general-purpose add-in expansion slots.  The model name of APA #2 is listed if it is included in this configuration.

columns.name: Available DIMM Slots
columns.description: The available dual in-line memory modules.

columns.name: Model Number or Config ID for Typical or Single Configuration
columns.description: The model or configuration number for the typical or single configuration of the qualified product family. It is defined as a product configuration that lies between the Minimum and Maximum Power configurations and is representative of a deployed product with high volume sales.

columns.name: Model Number or Config ID for High-end Performance Configuration
columns.description: The model or configuration number for the high-end performance configuration of the qualified product family. It is defined as the combination of Processor Socket Power, PSUs, Memory, Storage (HDD/SDD), and I/O devices that represents either the higher-price or higher-performance computing platform within the Product Family.

columns.name: Installed Processors for High-end Performance Configuration
columns.description: The number of processors installed in the product's as-shipped configuration.

columns.name: Cores Per Processor Low-end Performance Configuration
columns.description: The central processing unit package may include one or more processor cores.

columns.name: Cores Per Processor Typical or Single Configuration

columns.name: Num. of DDR Channels for High-end Performance Configuration
columns.description: The number of buffered double data rate (DDR) channels per Server/Blade/Node available in the configuration.  A buffered DDR channel is defined as a channel or Memory Port connecting a Memory Controller to a defined number of memory devices (e.g. DIMMs) in a computer server. A typical computer server may contain multiple Memory Controllers, which may in turn support one or more Buffered DDR Channels. As such, each Buffered DDR Channel serves only a fraction of the total addressable memory space in a computer server.

columns.name: Low-end Performance Configuration Redundant Power Supply
columns.description: A power supply unit (PSU) is a device that converts ac  input power to one or more dc power outputs for the purpose of powering a computer server. A computer server PSU must be self-contained and physically separable from the motherboard and must connect to the system via a removable or hard-wired electrical connection. A power supply unit can be installed explicitly for power redundancy in this configuration.

columns.name: Power Supply Type for Low-end Performance Configuration
columns.description: The power supply type can be either Single-output or Multi-output. A single-output power supply is a PSU that is designed to deliver the majority of its rated output power to one primary dc output for the purpose of powering a computer server. Single-output PSUs may offer one or more standby outputs that remain active whenever connected to an input power source. For purposes of this specification, the total rated power output from any additional PSU outputs that are not primary and standby outputs shall be no greater than 20 watts. A multi-output power supply is a PSU that is designed to deliver the majority of its rated output power to more than one primary dc output for the purpose of powering a computer server. Multi-output PSUs may offer one or more standby outputs that remain active whenever connected to an input power source. For purposes of this specification, the total rated power output from any additional PSU outputs that are not primary and standby outputs is greater than or equal to 20 watts

columns.name: Power Supply Type for High-end Performance Configuration
columns.description: The power supply type can be either Single-output or Multi-output. A single-output power supply is a PSU that is designed to deliver the majority of its rated output power to one primary dc output for the purpose of powering a computer server. Single-output PSUs may offer one or more standby outputs that remain active whenever connected to an input power source. For purposes of this specification, the total rated power output from any additional PSU outputs that are not primary and standby outputs shall be no greater than 20 watts. A multi-output power supply is a PSU that is designed to deliver the majority of its rated output power to more than one primary dc output for the purpose of powering a computer server. Multi-output PSUs may offer one or more standby outputs that remain active whenever connected to an input power source. For purposes of this specification, the total rated power output from any additional PSU outputs that are not primary and standby outputs is greater than or equal to 20 watts.

columns.name: SERT SORT Efficiency Score High-end Performance Configuration

columns.name: SERT SHA256 Efficiency Score Typical or Single Configuration

columns.name: SERT Flood Efficiency Score Low-end Performance Configuration

The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Product Processor Socket Count

columns.name: Resilient System
columns.description: A computer server designed with extensive Reliability, Availability, Serviceability (RAS) and scalability features integrated in the micro architecture of the system, CPU, and chipset.  See Appendix B of the Version 2.0 Computer Servers Program Requirements for more details on the definition of a Resilient System.

columns.name: APA #1 Model Name

columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Internal Volume (cu. ft.)
columns.description: Determined using straight-line segments following the gross interior dimensions of the appliance (Interior Volume = Interior Height x Interior Width x Interior Depth).

columns.name: Door Type

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description:
Designates the type of commercial coffee brewer, according to the following options: 
1) Small Batch Type II (24oz. - 128 oz.) 
2) Medium Batch Type II (>128 oz. - 256 oz.) 
3) Large Batch Type II (>256oz. - 384oz.) 
Refer to the specification in Section 1.K.a-c.

columns.name: Measured Average Tank Temperature in Ready-to-Brew Idle (F?)

columns.name: Maximum serving temperature during heavy-use brew test (°F)
columns.description: Maximum serving temperature during heavy-use brew test (°F)

columns.name: Energy Save Mode Feature
columns.description: An optional low power mode that is designed to reduce energy consumption during periods of non-use.

columns.name: Normalized Energy Save Mode Idle Energy Rate (watts/gal)
columns.description: The average rate of the coffee brewer energy consumption, after the energy save mode has been activated.

columns.name: Trans Storage Device 2 Raw Capacity (GB)
columns.description: The raw capacity of storage device 2 in GB.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream PSU Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to the PSU(s) by the manufacturer or private labeler.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's model number will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream PSUs 80PLUS Certification
columns.description: The 80PLUS certification level achieved by the PSU(s) installed.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's certification level will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream Other Devices Used for Cache
columns.description: If Other was chosen for Trans Device Used for Cache, the type of Other device is identified here.

columns.name: Stream Ratio Devices Mixed Optimal Point
columns.description: The ratio of installed storage devices with addressable storage (not used for cache) during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.  If there is more than one device in the highest predicted deployed storage device, the ratio of each storage device is separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream Installed Solid State Devices in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of Solid State Devices installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.  If there is more than one device in the highest predicted deployed storage device, each device's number of solid state drives is separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream Installed Rotational Devices in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of rotational devices installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.  If there is more than one device in the highest predicted deployed storage device, each device's number of rotational drives is separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream Num Redundant Controllers Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of redundant controllers installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Stream Automated Tiering Enabled During Testing Device 1
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the storage device 1.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Device 1 Optimal Point Seq Write Wrkld Test (MiBPS/W)
columns.description: The sequential write measurement for storage device 1 optimal point in MiB Per Second (MiBPS) per watt.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Storage Device 2 Type
columns.description: The type of storage device can be either a Hard Disk Drive (HDD) or a Solid State Drive (SSD).  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Physical or Modeled Data Device 2 Optimal Point
columns.description: The data for the storage device 2 optimal point is obtained by physically testing the system or through the use of modeled data.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Automated Tiering Enabled During Testing Device 2
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the storage device 2.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Device 2 Optimal Point Seq Write Wrkld Test (MiBPS/W)
columns.description: The sequential write measurement for storage device 2 optimal point in MiB Per Second (MiBPS) per watt.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: The SNIA Taxonomy is a categorization scheme for use in segmenting the data center storage market by end-use application and key product characteristics.  The three categories eligible for ENERGY STAR certification are Online 2, Online 3, and Online 4.

columns.name: Automated Storage Tiering Capable
columns.description: Automated Storage Tiering is the automatic movement of data between different types of storage devices. Tiering may occur within a single storage product or may span multiple storage products. For the purposes of ENERGY STAR certification, only tiering within a single storage product is applicable.

columns.name: Power and Temperature Reporting Additional Information
