columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to the model or configuration by the manufacturer or private labeler.  Stream indicates that this information is for the Streaming Workload Optimization Type.

columns.name: Stream Power Supply Unit (PSU) Type
columns.description: The type(s) of power supply unit(s) installed.  A Power Supply Unit (PSU) is a device that converts ac or dc input power to one or more dc power outputs for the purpose of powering a storage product. A storage PSU must be self-contained and physically separable from the system and must connect to the system via a removable or hard-wired electrical connection.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's type will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream PSU Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to the PSU(s) for sales and marketing purposes.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's model name will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream PSU Rated Output (W)
columns.description: The Rated Output per installed PSU in watts.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's rated output will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream Storage Device 1 Rated Speed (RPM)
columns.description: The rated speed of storage device 1 in RPM.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Device 1 Optimal Point Ready Idle Wrkld Test (GB/W)
columns.description: The ready idle measurement for storage device 1 optimal point in GB per watt.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Storage Device 2 Form Factor
columns.description: The form factor of storage device 2 is either 2.5 inch, 3.5 inch, or Other.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Storage Device 2 Rated Speed (RPM)
columns.description: The rated speed of storage device 2 in RPM.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Device 2 Optimal Point Ready Idle Wrkld Test (GB/W)
columns.description: The ready idle measurement for storage device 2 optimal point in GB per watt.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Storage Device 2 Rated Speed (RPM)
columns.description: The rated speed of storage device 2 in RPM.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Storage Device 2 Form Factor
columns.description: The form factor of storage device 2 is either 2.5 inch, 3.5 inch, or Other.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Storage Device 2 Type
columns.description: The type of storage device can be either a Hard Disk Drive (HDD) or a Solid State Drive (SSD).  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Device 1 Optimal Point Ready Idle Wrkld Test (GB/W)
columns.description: The ready idle measurement for storage device 1 optimal point in GB per watt.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Device 1 Optimal Point Random Write Wrkld Test (IOPS/W)
columns.description: The random write measurement for storage device 1 optimal point in I/O Per Second (IOPS) per watt.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Device 1 Optimal Point Random Read Wrkld Test (IOPS/W)
columns.description: The random read measurement for storage device 1 optimal point in I/O Per Second (IOPS) per watt.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Automated Tiering Enabled During Testing Device 1
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the storage device 1.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Physical or Modeled Data Device 1 Optimal Point
columns.description: The data for the storage device 1 optimal point is obtained by physically testing the system or through the use of modeled data.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Num Devices Storage Device 1 Optimal Point
columns.description: The number of devices installed in storage device 1 optimal point.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Storage Device 1 Raw Capacity (GB)
columns.description: The raw capacity of storage device 1 in GB.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Storage Device 1 Rated Speed (RPM)
columns.description: The rated speed of storage device 1 in RPM.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Optimal Point Ready Idle Workload Test (GB/W)
columns.description: The ready idle measurement for the highest predicted deployed storage device optimal point in GB per watt.

columns.name: Trans Optimal Point Random Write Workload Test (IOPS/W)
columns.description: The random write measurement for the highest predicted deployed storage device optimal point in IOPS per watt.

columns.name: Trans Auto Tiering Enabled Optimal Point
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Number of Redundant PSUs Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of redundant PSUs installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Number of PSUs Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of PSUs installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Num Redundant Controllers Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of redundant controllers installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Num of Controllers in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of controllers installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Installed Rotational Devices in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of rotational devices installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.  If there is more than one device in the highest predicted deployed storage device, each device's number of rotational drives is separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Trans Ratio Devices Mixed Optimal Point
columns.description: The ratio of installed storage devices with addressable storage (not used for cache) during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.  If there is more than one device in the highest predicted deployed storage device, the ratio of each storage device is separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Trans Total Num Installed Storage Devices Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of storage devices installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Devices Used for Cache
columns.description: The devices used for cache are identified here.  This can be DRAM, SSD, or Other device.

columns.name: Trans Total Amount of Cache (GB)
columns.description: The total amount of cache available in GB.  Cache is temporary storage used to transparently store transitory data and which is not directly addressable by end-user applications. Primarily used for expediting access to or from (typically) slower devices.

columns.name: Trans PSUs 80PLUS Certification
columns.description: The 80PLUS certification level achieved by the PSU(s) installed.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's certification level will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Trans PSU Rated Output (W)
columns.description: The Rated Output per installed PSU in watts.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's rated output will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Trans PSU Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to the PSU(s) by the manufacturer or private labeler.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's model number will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Trans Power Supply Unit (PSU) Type
columns.description: The type(s) of power supply unit(s) installed.  A Power Supply Unit (PSU) is a device that converts ac or dc input power to one or more dc power outputs for the purpose of powering a storage product. A storage PSU must be self-contained and physically separable from the system and must connect to the system via a removable or hard-wired electrical connection.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's type will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Trans Model Number or Config ID
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to the model or configuration by the manufacturer or private labeler.  Trans indicates that this information is for the Transaction Workload Optimization Type.

columns.name: Length of Rolling Average for Inlet Air Temperature (s)
columns.description: If configuration(s) is/are capable of calculating an inlet air temperature, the length of the rolling average is provided here.

columns.name: Length of Rolling Average for Input Power (s)
columns.description: If configuration(s) is/are capable of calculating an input power rolling average, the length of the rolling average is provided here.

columns.name: Active or Passive Cooling Enabled in Hardware on Shipment
columns.description: If Active Cooling, Passive Cooling, or both is enabled in hardware on shipment.

columns.name: Software Operating Environment Version
columns.description: Software operating environment/system version supported by storage configuration(s).

columns.name: Software Operating Environment Name
columns.description: Software operating environment/system name supported by storage configuration(s).

columns.name: Workload Optimization Type
columns.description: A configuration provided by the manufacturer may be optimized for the following workload types: transaction, streaming, or capacity.  Transaction is a workload type optimized for random I/O usage measured in I/O per second per watt.  Streaming is a workload type optimized primarily for sequential I/O usage, measured in MiB per second per watt.  Capacity is a workload type optimized for maximum storage as measured by GB (Raw Capacity) per watt.

columns.name: Storage Controller Manufacturer Name
columns.description: The manufacturer of the installed storage controller(s).

columns.name: Storage Controller Configuration
columns.description: A storage controller can be implemented in two different ways.  Scale-up storage is a storage product comprised of a discreet storage controller (with or without redundancy), which has a full view of all the storage devices in the storage product. Incremental storage capacity is added by the addition of storage devices under the control of the existing storage controller.  While Scale-out storage is a storage product capable of being comprised of two or more discreet storage controllers (with or without redundancy), combined with an overall integration or aggregation function resulting in a single storage product view for attached servers. Each discreet storage controller often has a partial view to a partition of the overall system's storage devices, but this is not a fixed requirement. Incremental storage capacity is added by the addition of storage devices under the control of the existing storage controller(s) and/or addition of additional storage devices along with additional controller(s).

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Stream Total Amount of Cache (GB)
columns.description: The total amount of cache available in GB.  Cache is temporary storage used to transparently store transitory data and which is not directly addressable by end-user applications. Primarily used for expediting access to or from (typically) slower devices.

columns.name: Stream Auto Tiering Enabled Optimal Point
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Stream Number of PSUs Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of PSUs installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Stream Total Num Installed Storage Devices Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of storage devices installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Device 1 Optimal Point Hot Band Wrkld Test (IOPS/W)
columns.description: The hot band measurement for storage device 1 optimal point in I/O Per Second (IOPS) per watt.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Automated Storage Tiering Enabled in Hardware on Shipment
columns.description: If Intelligent Power Distribution Unit (iPDU) is enabled in hardware on shipment.

columns.name: Trans Device 2 Optimal Point Ready Idle Wrkld Test (GB/W)
columns.description: The ready idle measurement for storage device 2 optimal point in GB per watt.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Device 2 Optimal Point Hot Band Wrkld Test (IOPS/W)
columns.description: The hot band measurement for storage device 2 optimal point in I/O Per Second (IOPS) per watt.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Automated Tiering Enabled During Testing Device 2
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the storage device 2.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Physical or Modeled Data Device 2 Optimal Point
columns.description: The data for the storage device 2 optimal point is obtained by physically testing the system or through the use of modeled data.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Num Devices Storage Device 2 Optimal Point
columns.description: The number of devices installed in storage device 2 optimal point.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream PSU Manufacturer Name
columns.description: The manufacturer of the installed PSU(s).  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's manufacturer name will be separated by a semicolon.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 2.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Data Center Storage that are effective as of March 15, 2021 or Version 2.1 requirements that are effective as of January 19, 2022. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/office_equipment/data_center_storage/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Vending Machines

columns.name: Conditions for Expected Use
columns.description: Indoor/outdoor machines may be suitable for outdoor use or suitable for protected locations. Indoor machines are suitable for indoor use only (per UL Standard 541).

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Qualified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for vending machines include R-290, R-600a, and R-744 (CO₂).

columns.name: Refrigerated Volume (cu. ft.)
columns.description: Refrigerated volume represents the volume of the interior cabinet where cold beverages are stored as measured by 10 CFR Part 431 Subpart Q, Appendix B.

columns.name: Vending Capacity (C)
columns.description: The maximum quantity of standard product that can be dispensed from one full loading of the vending machine without further reload operations when used as recommended by the manufacturer (per ANSI/ASHRAE 32.1).

columns.name: Model Number or Rebuilt Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Rebuilt Vending Machine
columns.description: A 'Yes' in this column indicates that the model is a rebuilt machine. The model was previously in use and subject to retrofitting or refurbishing for resale

columns.name: OEM Model Name
columns.description: For rebuilt machines, an identifier assigned by the OEM to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM)
columns.description: For rebuilt machines, name of the company that originally built the machine. This may or may not be the same organization that upgrades the machine to meet ENERGY STAR requirements.

columns.name: Door Type
columns.description: Vending machines eligible for ENERGY STAR may have a closed front door, glass front door, or live display front door.

columns.name: Energy Saving Component Type
columns.description: For rebuilt machines, the component type(s) used in the energy efficiency kit or as represented by the Component Model Numbers (e.g., fan assembly).

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Energy Saving Kit or Component OEM
columns.description: For rebuilt machines, the OEM of the energy efficiency component(s)/kit installed to meet ENERGY STAR requirements.

columns.name: Energy Use (kWh/day)
columns.description: The daily energy consumption value in kWh per day.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: The type of refrigerant used by the product.

columns.name: Energy Saving Kit or Component Model Number
columns.description: For rebuilt machines, the model number of the energy efficiency component(s)/kit installed to meet ENERGY STAR requirements.

columns.name: Type of Power Controls
columns.description: Indicates which low power modes are supported by the product.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Accessories Covered by Lower Power Mode
columns.description: For models with an Accessory Low Power Mode, the accessories which are equipped with a low power mode.

columns.name: OEM Model Number
columns.description: For rebuilt machines, a distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the OEM.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: Refrigerated beverage vending machines are either Class A or Class B. Combination vending machines are either Combination A or Combination B. 'A' types have mostly transparent fronts; 'B' types have mostly opaque fronts. Combination machines may contain two or more compartments separated by a solid partition. For detailed definitions see the RBVM product specification.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 4.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Refrigerated Beverage Vending Machines that are effective as of April 29, 2020. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/other/vending_machines/key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Light Commercial HVAC

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the type of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: COP Rating at 17°F
columns.description: A measure of efficiency in the heating mode that represents the ratio of total heating capacity to electrical energy input, measured at 17 degrees F.

columns.name: Heating Capacity at 5°F (kBtu/h)
columns.description: The ability of the system to deliver sensible and latent heating when the outside air temperature is 5 F.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: COP Rating at 5°F
columns.description: A measure of efficiency in the heating mode that represents the ratio of total heating capacity to electrical energy input, measured at 5 degrees F.

columns.name: HSPF Rating (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: The total heating output of a central air-conditioning heat pump during its normal annual usage period for heating, expressed in Btu's and divided by the total electric power input, expressed in Btu/Wh using 10 CFR part 431, Subpart F, §431.96, Table 1.

columns.name: SEER2 Rating (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: The ratio of the total heat removed from the conditioned space during the annual cooling season to the total electrical energy consumed by the air conditioner or heat pump during the same season, expressed in Btu/Wh using Proposed Appendix B1 at 86 FR 70316, 70346-70347 (Dec. 9, 2021).

columns.name: SEER Rating (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: The ratio of the total heat removed from the conditioned space during the annual cooling season to the total electrical energy consumed by the air conditioner or heat pump during the same season, expressed in Btu/Wh using 10 CFR part 431, Subpart F, §431.96, Table 1.

columns.name: EER2 Rating (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: The ratio of the produced cooling effect of an air conditioner or heat pump to its net work input, expressed in Btu/watt-hour using Proposed Appendix B1 at 86 FR 70316, 70346-70347 (Dec. 9, 2021).

columns.name: EER Rating (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: The ratio of the produced cooling effect of an air conditioner or heat pump to its net work input, expressed in Btu/watt-hour using 10 CFR part 431, Subpart F, §431.96, Table 1.

columns.name: Meets Peak Cooling Requirements
columns.description: Meets EER2 requirements for efficient cooling at 95F.

columns.name: Heating Section Type
columns.description: The type of heating section the LCHVAC systems uses. Central air conditioners and heat pumps systems can use either all other heating section types (propane, gas, oil), electric resistance, or none. VRF Multi-Split systems either come with heat recovery or without heat recovery.

columns.name: Heating Capacity at 47°F (kBtu/h)
columns.description: The ability of the system to deliver sensible and latent heating when the outside air temperature is 47 F.

columns.name: Cooling Capacity (kBtu/h)
columns.description: The capacity associated with the change in air enthalpy between the air entering the unit and the air leaving the unit, which includes both the latent (change in humidity ratio) and sensible (change in dry-bulb temperature) capacities expressed in Btu/h and include the heat of circulation fan(s) and motor(s).

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Cold Climate
columns.description: Indicates heat pumps that meet the ENERGY STAR cold climate criteria; cold climate heat pumps will provide better heating performance in low ambient temperatures, making these products an excellent choice for cold climate installations where the heat pump will be the sole heating source.

columns.name: Split System or Single Package
columns.description: This column identifies whether the system uses a split system arrangement (remote condensing unit) or a single package system (all components are stored in a single cabinet). All VRF models are required to be listed as "Split System".

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for light commercial HVAC include R-32 and R-454B.

columns.name: COP Rating at 47°F
columns.description: A measure of efficiency in the heating mode that represents the ratio of total heating capacity to electrical energy input, measured at 47 degrees F.

columns.name: HSPF2 Rating (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: The total heating output of a central air-conditioning heat pump during its normal annual usage period for heating, expressed in Btu's and divided by the total electric power input, expressed in Btu/Wh using Proposed Appendix B1 at 86 FR 70316, 70346-70347 (Dec. 9, 2021).

columns.name: IEER Rating (Btu/Wh)
columns.description: IEER is a measure that expresses cooling part-load EER efficiency for commercial air-conditioners, heat pumps, and VRF Multi-Splits on the basis of weighted operation at various load capacities using 10 CFR part 431, Subpart F, §431.96, Table 1.

columns.name: Heating Capacity at 17°F (kBtu/h)
columns.description: The ability of the system to deliver sensible and latent heating when the outside air temperature is 17 F.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: An air conditioner model consists of one or more factory-made assemblies that normally include an evaporator or cooling coil(s), compressor(s), and condenser(s) to provide the function of air cooling. A heat pump model is similar but also provides the function of air heating. Air conditioners and heat pumps may additionally include the functions of air circulation, air cleaning, dehumidifying, or humidifying. A variable refrigerant flow multi-split (VRF) model consists of a unit of commercial package air-conditioning and heating equipment that is configured as a split system incorporating a single refrigerant circuit, with one or more outdoor units, at least one variable-speed compressor or an alternate compressor combination for varying the capacity of the system by three or more steps, and multiple indoor fan coil units, each of which is individually metered and individually controlled by an integral control device and common communications network and which can operate independently in response to multiple indoor thermostats. Very Small: <65,000 BTU/h Small: ≥65,000 BTU/h and <135,000 BTU/h Large: ≥135,000 BTU/h and <240,000 BTU/h

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit. For light commercial HVAC models, model number indicates the outdoor model number.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 4.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Light Commercial HVAC that are effective as of January 1, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/heating_cooling/light_commercial_heating_cooling/key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Water Heaters

columns.name: Water Heater Depth (inches)
columns.description: The water heater's depth, in inches.

columns.name: Water Heater Height (inches)
columns.description: The water heater's height, in inches.

columns.name: Maximum Gallons Per Minute
columns.description: The maximum amount of hot water, in gallons, a tankless water heater can supply per minute over a 67°F rise.

columns.name: DR Protocol
columns.description: Indicates which standardized protocol is used to send load management messages to the unit.

columns.name: Time of Use Schedule
columns.description: Indicates if a water heater is capable of storing and operating based on Time of Use rates

columns.name: Primary Communication Module/Device Brand Name and Model Number
columns.description:
An external communication 
module or device is required if
communication hardware is not built into the unit.

columns.name: Is Broadband Internet Connection Needed for Demand Response?
columns.description: Describes whether the product can participate in utility demand response programs without the home having a broadband connection.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the type of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: System can communicate using these protocols for sending messages and requests, some of which work wirelessly and some with wires (open standard media/network layer).

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Heat pump water heaters require refrigerants as well. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for heat pump water heaters include R-744 (CO₂).

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: This field is used to indicate the type of qualified product: Gas Storage Water Heater, Gas Tankless Water Heater, Heat Pump Water Heater, High Capacity Storage, Solar with Gas Backup, or Solar with Electric Backup.

columns.name: Tank Height (inches)
columns.description: Tank height is the distance from the bottom of the water heater’s tank to the top of its tank.

columns.name: Solar System Type
columns.description: Forced Circulation systems use a pump to circulate the water or other fluid from the collector where it is heated by the sun to the storage tank. Integral Collector Storage (ICS) systems, or “batch” water heaters, combine the collector and the storage tank into one. Thermosyphon systems have a separate storage tank, located above the collector. Liquid (which could be water or an antifreeze solution) warmed in the collector rises to the storage tank.

columns.name: Solar Uniform Energy Factor (SUEF)
columns.description: Solar Uniform Energy Factor (SUEF) refers to the energy delivered by the total system divided by the electrical or gas energy put into the system. A value of 99 indicates that under rating conditions, the system used no gas or electrical energy to heat water.

columns.name: Solar Tank Volume (gallons)
columns.description: Capacity of the tank, in gallons.

columns.name: Connected Capable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model meets optional connected criteria as defined in the specification, which may include functions helpful to the electrical grid (standardized signals and responses to manage energy use) and those helpful to consumers, such as scheduling and energy reporting.

columns.name: Communication Hardware Architecture
columns.description: The Hardware Architecture describes the type of module/device within or external to the system needed for connection.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Water Heater Width (inches)
columns.description: The water heater's width, in inches.

columns.name: Minimum Gallons Per Minute
columns.description: The minimum amount of hot water, in gallons, a tankless water heater can supply per minute over a 67°F rise.

columns.name: Draw Pattern (Intended Usage)
columns.description: Draw pattern used per the Appendix E test method. For storage-type water heaters, draw pattern is determined by the FHR test. For gas tankless water heaters, draw pattern is determined per Max. GPM rating test. For all solar water heaters,  draw pattern is determined by collector size.

columns.name: Uniform Energy Factor (UEF)
columns.description: Uniform Energy Factor (UEF) is the newest measure of water heater overall efficiency. It is a metric used to compare the relative efficiencies of water heaters. The higher the UEF is, the more efficient the water heater.

columns.name: Electric Usage (kWh/yr)
columns.description: Electric usage per year per

columns.name: Therms/year for Natural Gas
columns.description: Therms/year refers to the amount of natural gas consumed by the water heater with a tested outlet temperature of 125°.

columns.name: Upper Compressor Cut Off Temperature for HPWH (°F)
columns.description: Requested if Product Type is Heat Pump. Include the ambient temperature above which the compressor cuts off and electric resistance only operation begins.

columns.name: Max. Amps
columns.description: Maximum amperage

columns.name: Recovery Efficiency (%)
columns.description: Recovery efficiency refers to how efficiently the heat from the burner is transferred to the water in the tank.

columns.name: Height to Vent (inches)
columns.description: Height to vent is the distance from the bottom of the water heater’s tank to its vent.

columns.name: Vent Size 1 (inches)
columns.description: Vent size is the width or diameter of the vent opening.

columns.name: Solar Collector Panel Area (sq feet)
columns.description: Refers to the total area consumed by the solar collector.

columns.name: OG-300 Number
columns.description: The OG-300 certification number is assigned by the Solar Rating & Certification Corporation.

columns.name: Freeze Tolerance Limit (F)
columns.description: The limit to the system’s tolerance of freezing weather conditions.

columns.name: Solar Type of Backup
columns.description: This field is used to indicate whether the backup to the solar system is a tank or is tankless.

columns.name: Solar Auxiliary Tank Volume (gallons)
columns.description: Volume is the capacity of the tank in a solar system, in gallons.

columns.name: Solar Auxiliary Tank Input (kW)
columns.description: Electric Input kW refers to the input wattage of the heat pump or supplementary electric elements.

columns.name: Load Up Functionality
columns.description: Indicates if a Connected product responds with a Basic Load Up and/or Advanced Load Up.

columns.name: Optional Operational Mode Functionality
columns.description: Indicates if a product is capable of responding to Set Point Adjustment and/or Relative Price Signal commands.

columns.name: Sound Pressure Level
columns.description: Sound pressure level as measured per the NEEA Electric or Gas-Fueled Advanced Water Heating Specification, in dBA

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Gallons/year for Propane
columns.description: Gallons/year refers to the amount of propane consumed by the water heater with a tested outlet temperature of 125°F.

columns.name: Max. Input Rate for Gas Products (Btu/hr)
columns.description: The rate at which energy is consumed by the gas water heater when running at maximum capacity, in BTU/hr.

columns.name: Electric Input (kW)
columns.description: Rated electrical input power.

columns.name: Input Voltage for HPWH (V)
columns.description: Voltage required for a heat pump water heater.

columns.name: Lower Compressor Cut Off Temperature for HPWH (°F)
columns.description: Required if Product Type is Heat Pump. Include the ambient temperature below which the compressor cuts off and electric resistance only operation begins.

columns.name: Tax Credit Eligible
columns.description: Indicates product model is eligible for federal income tax credits available under the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022.

columns.name: Fuel
columns.description: Fuel type refers the kind of fuel the water heater uses to heat the water, either natural gas or propane.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Vent Type
columns.description: The type of vent used by the water heater: Atmospheric Vent, Direct Vent, or Power Vent.

columns.name: Storage Volume (gallons)
columns.description: Volume is the capacity of the tank, in gallons. Storage Volume is not applicable for gas tankless, solar with gas backup, or solar with electric backup water heaters.

columns.name: First Hour Rating (gallons)
columns.description: First Hour Rating is the amount of hot water (in gallons) a storage water heater can supply in the first hour of using water, starting with a tank full of hot water.

columns.name: Heat Pump Type
columns.description: Standard HPWH require 240 Volt service, but certain products are appropriate for installation on a 120 Volt, 15 Amp Circuit. Split-System heat pump water heaters are residential water heaters where the compressor, evaporator, and/or condenser are separated from the storage tank.

columns.name: Tank Diameter (inches)
columns.description: Tank diameter is the width or diameter of the water heater’s tank.

columns.name: Vent Size 2 (inches)
columns.description: Vent size 2 is the width or diameter of a second vent opening.

columns.name: Solar Collector Type
columns.description: Solar Water Heaters are available in the following collector types: batch, flat-plate, and evacuated tube. Batch collectors heat water in dark tanks or tubes within an insulated box, storing the water until drawn. Flat-plate collectors consist of copper tubes fitted to flat absorber plates. Evacuated tubes contain a vacuum of space around the metal or glass tube.

columns.name: Solar Auxiliary Tank Input (BTU/hr)
columns.description: Auxiliary Input refers to the input BTU per hour rating of the backup gas water heater in the solar system.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 5.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Water Heaters that are effective April 18, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/water_heaters/residential_water_heaters_key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Commercial Water Heaters

columns.name: Optional Status and Messaging Features
columns.description:
All of these features are optional for ENERGY STAR Partners to report to EPA.
The four features include: Fault Detection and Display - the equipment reports, in plain text, system faults that have occurred; Performance Reporting - the equipment is capable of reporting at least three performance metrics such as fuel usage, hot water use, etc.; Energy Savings Reporting - the equipment is capable of analyzing performance to report potential energy savings opportunities to the user; Predictive Maintenance - the equipment is capable of tracking water heater use and wear rates to predict and alert the user of potential maintenance requirements.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Additional Model Information

Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Tank Height (in.)
columns.description: Tank height is the distance from the bottom of the water heater's tank to the top of its tank.

columns.name: Standby Loss (Btu/hr)
columns.description: The average hourly energy, expressed in Btu/hr, required to maintain the stored water temperature.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: This field is used to indicate the type of qualified product: Gas Storage Water Heater, Gas Storage-type Tankless Water Heater, Gas Tankless Water Heater, or Electric Heat Pump Water Heater.

columns.name: Fuel Type
columns.description: Fuel type refers to the kind of fuel that the water heater uses to heat the water, either natural gas or propane for gas-fired water heaters, or electric for heat pump water heaters.

columns.name: Vent Type
columns.description: The type of vent used by the water heater: Atmospheric Vent, Direct Vent, or Power Vent.

columns.name: Storage Volume (gallons)
columns.description: The amount of water contained in the water heater measured in gallons. Some tankless water heaters do not have a storage volume.

columns.name: Height to Vent (in.)
columns.description: Height to vent is the distance from the bottom of the water heater's tank to its vent.

columns.name: Tank Diameter (in.)
columns.description: Tank diameter is the width or diameter of the water heater’s tank.

columns.name: Vent Size (in.)
columns.description: Vent size is the width or diameter of the vent opening.

columns.name: Field No Longer Used
columns.description: This field previously included information that was determined to no longer be relevant.

columns.name: Input Rate  - Gas-fired (kBtu/hr)
columns.description: Input refers to the burner size of the gas-fired water heater, in thousand BTU per hour.

columns.name: Thermal Efficiency (TE)
columns.description: The ratio of the heat transferred to the water flowing through the water heater to the amount of energy consumed by the water heater.

columns.name: Electric Input - Electric Heat Pump (kW)
columns.description: Electric Input refers to the input wattage of the heat pump and ancillary electric components (e.g., fans, blowers, storage tank, piping, and controls).

columns.name: Coefficient of Performance (COPh)
columns.description: The dimensionless ratio of the useful heat transfer gained by the water to the rate of electrical power consumed during full input rate operation.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Vent Size 2 (in.)
columns.description: Vent size 2 is the width or diameter of a second vent opening.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 2.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Commercial Water Heaters that are effective as of October 1, 2018. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/water_heaters/commercial_water_heaters/key_product_criteria .

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 1.1 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Large Network Equipment that are effective as of March 1, 2016. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/office_equipment/large_network_equipment/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Uninterruptible Power Supplies

columns.name: Manufacturer Take Back Program
columns.description: Indicates whether the manufacturer offers a UPS take-back program in the U.S.

columns.name: Communication Protocols
columns.description: Indicates the communication protocols available on the model and/or the meter.

columns.name: Modular UPS Module Tested Model Number
columns.description: For Modular UPS,  indicates the model or part number of the installed module(s) in the specific tested model’s chassis of the unit under test.

columns.name: Total Input Power in W at 0% Load Max Config Highest Dependency (ac)
columns.description:
The ratio of active input power to the apparent input power of the maximum configuration model operating at 100 percent rated load in the highest-input dependency normal mode of a Multiple-normal-mode Ac-output UPS.

This column does not apply to Dc-output UPSs which are tested in a single mode only.

columns.name: Weighted Efficiency Calc Min Config Lowest Dependency
columns.description: The calculated average efficiency of the minimum configuration model operating at specified percentages of the rated load. The average efficiency is weighted to reflect the expected utilization of the UPS. For a Multiple-normal-mode Ac-output UPS, the model is supplying power to the load in its lowest-input dependency normal mode (i.e., VFD).

columns.name: Efficiency at 100% Load Min Config Lowest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac minimum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Efficiency at 50% Load Min Config Highest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac minimum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Efficiency at 50% Load Min Config Lowest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac minimum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Efficiency at 25% Load Min Config Lowest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac minimum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Test Output Voltage (V)
columns.description: The output voltage used during the ENERGY STAR test.

columns.name: Number of Normal Modes
columns.description:
Single-normal-mode UPS: A UPS that functions in Normal Mode within the parameters of only one set of input dependency characteristics. For example, a UPS that functions only as VFI.
Multiple-normal-mode UPS: A UPS that functions in Normal Mode within the parameters of more than one set of input dependency characteristics. For example, a UPS that can function as either VFI or VFD.

columns.name: Height (mm)
columns.description: The physical height of the model in millimeters, not including any external energy storage system with separate enclosures.

columns.name: Minimum Configuration Tested Model Number
columns.description: The model number of the specific tested single configuration model of a non-Modular UPS or the minimum configuration model of a Modular UPS.  A Modular UPS is comprised of two or more single UPS modules, sharing one or more common frames and a common energy storage system, whose outputs, in normal mode of operation, are connected to a common output bus contained entirely within the frame(s). Modular UPSs may be used to provide redundancy, to scale capacity or both. For qualification of a Modular UPS Product Family, both the minimum and maximum configuration models are tested.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: The type of power output supplied by the UPS classified as Alternating Current (Ac)-output UPS with a continuous flow of electrical charge that periodically reverses direction and Direct Current (Dc)-output UPS/Rectifier with a continuous flow of electric charge that is unidirectional.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Efficiency at 50% Load Minimum Configuration (dc)
columns.description: For dc minimum configuration models, the calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data.

columns.name: Number of Surge Protected Only Outlets
columns.description: Indicates the total number of surge protected only outlets provided by the model.

columns.name: Total Number of Outlets
columns.description: Indicates the total number of outlets/output connections provided by the model.

columns.name: Total Input Power in W at 0% Load Minimum Configuration (dc)
columns.description: The Total Real Input Power at 0% load in W for the minimum configuration dc model.

columns.name: Test Output Frequency (Hz)
columns.description: The output frequency used during the ENERGY STAR test.

columns.name: Test Input Frequency (Hz)
columns.description: The input frequency used during the ENERGY STAR test.

columns.name: Test Input Voltage (V rms)
columns.description: The input voltage used during the ENERGY STAR test.

columns.name: Modular UPS
columns.description: A UPS comprised of two or more single UPS units, sharing one or more common frames and a common energy storage system, whose outputs, in Normal Mode of operation, are connected to a common output bus contained entirely within the frame(s).

columns.name: Number of Battery Backup and Surge Protected Outlets
columns.description: Indicates the total number of battery backup and surge protected outlets provided by the model.

columns.name: Rated Output Frequency (Hz)
columns.description: Indicates the output frequency (Hz) range for which the UPS is rated (i.e. nameplate value) to operate.

columns.name: Rated Input Frequency (Hz)
columns.description: Indicates the input frequency (Hz) range for which the UPS is rated (i.e. nameplate value) to operate.

columns.name: Topology (ac)
columns.description: Indicates the basic design of the model.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Model Web Page URL
columns.description: A web URL for more information about the certified model.

columns.name: Default Normal Mode (ac)
columns.description: Indicates the default Normal Mode of the model in its as-shipped configuration.

columns.name: Energy Storage System Removable to Another Room
columns.description: Indicates whether the energy storage system can be removed from the UPS for placement in another room, to allow the placement of the UPS in an unconditioned space, saving energy.

columns.name: Efficiency at 30% Load Minimum Configuration (dc)
columns.description: For dc minimum configuration models, the calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Power Conversion Mechanism
columns.description: The power conversion mechanism classified as Static UPS where solid-state power electronic components provide the output voltage and Rotary UPS where one or more electrical rotating machines provide the output voltage.

columns.name: Active Output Power Rating Minimum Configuration (W)
columns.description: The actual power supplied to the maximum load for which the minimum configuration model is designed or rated to support.

columns.name: Apparent Output Power Rating Minimum Configuration (VA)
columns.description: The product of the current and voltage of the circuit operating at the maximum load the minimum configuration model is designed or rated to support.

columns.name: Maximum Configuration Tested Model Number
columns.description: The model number of the maximum configuration tested model for the qualification of a Modular UPS Product Family.

columns.name: Active Output Power Rating Maximum Configuration (W)
columns.description: The actual power supplied to the maximum load for which the maximum configuration model is designed or rated to support.

columns.name: Apparent Output Power Rating Maximum Configuration (VA)
columns.description: The product of the current and voltage of the circuit operating at the maximum load the maximum configuration model is designed or rated to support.

columns.name: Topology and Product Type Combined
columns.description: Indicates the basic design of the model.

columns.name: Application
columns.description: Indicates the intended application(s) for the model. Consumer models are intended to protect desktop computers and related peripherals, and/or home entertainment devices such as TVs, set-top boxes, DVRs, Blu-ray and DVD players. Commercial models are intended to protect small business and branch office information and communication technology equipment such as servers, network switches and routers, and small storage arrays. Data Center models are intended to protect large installations of information and communication technology equipment such as enterprise servers, networking equipment, and large storage arrays.

columns.name: Rated Input Voltage (V rms)
columns.description: Indicates the input voltage range (V rms) for which the UPS is rated (i.e. nameplate value) to operate.

columns.name: Rated Output Voltage (V)
columns.description: Indicates the output voltage (V) range for which the UPS is rated (i.e. nameplate value) to operate.

columns.name: Rack Mountable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model can be mounted to a rack typically found in server closets, data centers, and telecommunications offices.

columns.name: Rack Mount Height (U)
columns.description: Indicates the rack mount height for the model in rack units or “U”, equal to 1.75 in (44.45 mm).

columns.name: Width (mm)
columns.description: The physical width of the model in millimeters, not including any external energy storage system with separate enclosures.

columns.name: Depth (mm)
columns.description: The physical depth of the model in millimeters, not including any external energy storage system with separate enclosures.

columns.name: Normal Mode(s) Input Dependency Characteristic (ac)
columns.description: The capability of an Ac-output UPS to operate in one or more Normal Modes classified by input dependency characteristic (ordered from highest to lowest input dependency): Voltage and Frequency Dependent (VFD): Capable of protecting the load from power outage Voltage Independent (VI): Capable of protecting the load as required for VFD, above, and in addition from under-voltage applied continuously to the input and over-voltage applied continuously to the input Voltage and Frequency Independent (VFI): Independent of voltage and frequency variations and capable of protecting the load against adverse effects from such variation without depleting the stored energy source. Input dependency characteristic is not applicable to Dc-output UPS/Rectifier models.

columns.name: Total Input Power in W at 0% Load Min Config Lowest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: The Total Real Input Power at 0% Load in W for the minimum configuration model in the lowest input dependency mode.

columns.name: Total Input Power in W at 0% Load Min Config Highest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: The Total Real Input Power at 0% Load in W for the minimum configuration model in the highest input dependency mode. Required only for multiple-normal-mode UPSs.

columns.name: Efficiency at 25% Load Min Config Highest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac minimum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Efficiency at 75% Load Min Config Lowest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac minimum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Efficiency at 75% Load Min Config Highest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac minimum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Efficiency at 100% Load Min Config Highest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac minimum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Weighted Efficiency Calc Min Config Highest Dependency
columns.description:
The calculated average efficiency of the minimum configuration Multiple-normal-mode Ac-output UPS model operating in the highest-input dependency normal mode (i.e., VFI or VI) at specified percentages of the rated load. The average efficiency is weighted to reflect the expected utilization of the UPS.

This column does not apply to Dc-output UPSs which are tested in a single mode only.

columns.name: Efficiency at 40% Load Minimum Configuration (dc)
columns.description: For dc minimum configuration models, the calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data.

columns.name: Efficiency at 60% Load Minimum Configuration (dc)
columns.description: For dc minimum configuration models, the calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data.

columns.name: Efficiency at 80% Load Minimum Configuration (dc)

columns.name: Minimum Configuration Input Power Factor Lowest-Input Dependency
columns.description: The ratio of active input power to apparent input power of the minimum configuration model operating at 100 percent rated load in the lowest-input dependency normal mode.

columns.name: Minimum Configuration Input Power Factor Highest-Input Dependency
columns.description:
The ratio of active input power to apparent input power of the minimum configuration model operating at 100 percent rated load in the highest-input dependency normal mode of a Multiple-normal-mode Ac-output UPS.

This column does not apply to Dc-output UPSs which are tested in a single mode only.

columns.description: The ratio of active input power to apparent input power of the maximum configuration model operating at 100 percent rated load in the lowest-input dependency normal mode.

columns.description: For ac maximum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.description: For ac maximum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Efficiency at 100% Load Max Config Lowest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac maximum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

columns.name: Efficiency at 100% Load Max Config Highest Dependency (ac)
columns.description: For ac maximum configuration models, the  calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data at the following loads: 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. For multiple-normal-mode UPSs, the efficiency in the highest dependency is also reported.

The average efficiency is weighted to reflect the expected utilization of the UPS. For a Multiple-normal-mode Ac-output UPS, the model is supplying power to the load in its lowest-input dependency normal mode (i.e., VFD).

The average efficiency is weighted to reflect the expected utilization of the UPS.

columns.name: Total Input Power in W at 0% Load Maximum Configuration  (dc)
columns.description: The Total Real Input Power at 0% load in W for the maximum configuration dc model.

columns.name: Efficiency at 30% Load Maximum Configuration (dc)
columns.description: For dc maximum configuration models, the calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data.

columns.description: For dc maximum configuration models, the calculated efficiency expressed as a decimal based on measured test data.

columns.name: Maximum Configuration Input Power Factor Lowest-Input Dependency
