This column does not apply to Dc-output UPSs which are tested in a single mode only.

columns.name: Efficiency (%)
columns.description: The calculated average efficiency of the model or system based on measured power data. The average efficiency is weighted to reflect the expected utilization of the UPS.

columns.name: Energy Storage Mechanism
columns.description: The mechanism of energy storage to provide power when the ac input power is disconnected or is out of the required tolerance range.

columns.name: Energy Storage System Technology
columns.description: Indicates the technology/chemistry of the energy storage system.

columns.name: Energy Storage System Configuration
columns.description: Indicates whether the energy storage system is integral to the model (i.e. installed in the chassis) or contained in a separate enclosure.

columns.name: Energy Storage System Runtime at 100% Load (min.)
columns.description: Indicates the runtime at 100% resistive load of the energy storage system tested with the model in minutes.

columns.name: Energy Storage System Runtime at 50% Load (min.)
columns.description: Indicates the runtime at 50% resistive load of the energy storage system tested with the model in minutes.

columns.name: Energy Storage System Warranty (yrs)
columns.description: The manufacturer warranty provided for the energy storage system tested with the model.

columns.name: Energy Storage System Information URL
columns.description: A web URL to additional information about the energy storage system, such as configuration options; capacities; runtimes, etc. provided by the ENERGY STAR Partner or an energy storage system vendor.

columns.name: Battery Recycling URL
columns.description: A web URL for information about battery recycling programs.

columns.name: Network Connections Available
columns.description: Indicates the available network connections for management of the model.

columns.name: Communication Protocol Other
columns.description: Specifies the type of communication protocol if other.

columns.name: Manufacturer Take Back Program URL
columns.description: A web URL to manufacturer take-back program if available.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 2.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Uninterruptible Power Supplies that are effective as of January 1, 2019. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/office_equipment/uninterruptible_power_supplies/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Storm Windows

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Date Available on the market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Solar Transmittance
columns.description: The amount of solar energy (heat) that passes through the storm window. Solar transmittance ranges from 0 to 1. Generally, higher solar transmittance values are preferable in colder climates and lower values are preferable in warmer climates.

columns.name: Weep Holes
columns.description: Feature that allows moisture to escape from between the storm window and primary window.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: Whether the product is an Exterior or Interior Storm Window product.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Storm Window Zone
columns.description: The storm window is only considered ENERGY STAR certified when installed in this climate zone. Climate zones share general climatic conditions, such as the number of heating days and cooling days, and are indicated by a shaded area on the ENERGY STAR storm window label.

columns.name: Frame Material
columns.description: The material used for the frame of the storm window.

columns.name: Screen included
columns.description: Is a screen included in the standard version of this storm window model?

columns.name: Screen Size
columns.description: Amount of the total opening covered by the screen.

columns.name: Alternate  Moisture Management
columns.description: The storm window's moisture management system. Please note, a moisture management system is not required for ENERGY STAR certified interior storm windows.

columns.name: Weather Stripping included
columns.description: Whether or not weather stripping is included with this product.

columns.name: Thermal breaks for metal frame mounting included
columns.description: Whether or not thermal breaks for metal frame mounting is included with this product.

columns.name: Manufacturer Warranty Period
columns.description: The length of the warranty period after purchase.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: This list/dataset includes products sold in the U.S..

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Operator type
columns.description: How the window opens and closes.

columns.name: Number of tracks
columns.description: The number of tracks of the storm window.

columns.name: Frame Finished (painted or coated)
columns.description: Is the frame painted or coated?

columns.name: Glazing Material
columns.description: The material used for the window (generally glass, coated glass, or plastic).

columns.name: Number of glazing layers
columns.description: Number of layers of glass or plastic used for the storm window. Most storm windows use one glazing layer.

columns.name: Emissivity
columns.description: The ability of the storm window to reflect heat inside instead of emitting outside. Emissivity ranges from 0 to 1, with a lower value reflecting more heat and being more efficient.

columns.name: Manufacturer Warranty
columns.description: Whether or not the manufacturer offers a warranty for this product.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: AERC 1.2 Air Leakage Rating
columns.description: The rate of air passing through the storm window when mounted over a calibrated panel, expressed in cubic feet per minute per square foot (cfm/ft2). Lower air leakage ratings mean that less air passes through the storm window.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 1.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Storm Windows that are effective as of September 5, 2018. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/building_products/storm_windows_key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Data Center Storage - File I/O

columns.name: Stream Power Supply Unit (PSU) Type
columns.description: The type(s) of power supply unit(s) installed.  A Power Supply Unit (PSU) is a device that converts ac or dc input power to one or more dc power outputs for the purpose of powering a storage product. A storage PSU must be self-contained and physically separable from the system and must connect to the system via a removable or hard-wired electrical connection.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's type will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Comp Storage Device 2 Type
columns.description: The type of storage device can be either a Hard Disk Drive (HDD) or a Solid State Drive (SSD).  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Comp Num Devices Storage Device 1 Optimal Point
columns.description: The number of devices installed in storage device 1 optimal point.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Comp Storage Device 1 Raw Capacity (GiB)
columns.description: The raw capacity of storage device 1 in GB.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Comp Storage Device 1 Rated Speed (RPM)
columns.description: The rated speed of storage device 1 in RPM.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Comp Optimal Point Ready Idle Workload Test (GB/W)
columns.description: The ready idle measurement for the highest predicted deployed storage device optimal point in GB per watt.

columns.name: Comp Optimal Point SW Build  Workload Test
columns.description: The SW Build measurement for the highest predicted deployed storage device optimal point

columns.name: Comp Auto Tiering Enabled Optimal Point
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Comp Number of PSUs Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of PSUs installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Comp Num of Controllers in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of controllers installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Stream Automated Tiering Enabled During Testing Device 1
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the storage device 1.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Device 1 Optimal Point Ready Idle Wrkld Test (GB/W)
columns.description: The ready idle measurement for the highest predicted deployed storage device optimal point in GB per watt.

columns.name: Stream Storage Device 2 Rated Speed (RPM)
columns.description: The rated speed of storage device 2 in RPM.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Physical or Modeled Data Device 2 Optimal Point
columns.description: The data for the storage device 2 optimal point is obtained by physically testing the system or through the use of modeled data.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Comp Model Number or Config ID
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to the model or configuration by the manufacturer or private labeler.

columns.name: Comp PSU Manufacturer Name
columns.description: The manufacturer of the installed PSU(s).

columns.name: Comp PSU Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to the PSU(s) for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Comp PSU Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to the PSU(s) by the manufacturer or private labeler.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's model number will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Comp PSU Rated Output (W)
columns.description: The Rated Output per installed PSU in watts.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's rated output will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Comp Total Amount of Cache (GiB)
columns.description: The total amount of cache available in GB.  Cache is temporary storage used to transparently store transitory data and which is not directly addressable by end-user applications. Primarily used for expediting access to or from (typically) slower devices.

columns.name: Comp Devices Used for Cache
columns.description: The devices used for cache are identified here.  This can be DRAM, SSD, or Other device.

columns.name: Comp Other Devices Used for Cache
columns.description: If Other was chosen for Trans Device Used for Cache, the type of Other device is identified here.

columns.name: Comp Installed Rotational Devices in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of rotational devices installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.  If there is more than one device in the highest predicted deployed storage device, each device's number of rotational drives is separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Capacity Optimizing Methods (COMs)
columns.description: Capacity Optimizing Methods (COMs) are the reduction of data stored on storage devices through a combination of hardware and / or software.  Common COMs include thin provisioning, data deduplication, compression, and delta snapshots.

columns.name: Workload Optimization Type
columns.description: A configuration provided by the manufacturer may be optimized for the following workload types: transaction, streaming, or capacity.  Transaction is a workload type optimized for random I/O usage measured in I/O per second per watt.  Streaming is a workload type optimized primarily for sequential I/O usage, measured in MiB per second per watt.  Capacity is a workload type optimized for maximum storage as measured by GB (Raw Capacity) per watt.

columns.name: Automated Storage Tiering Capable
columns.description: Automated Storage Tiering is the automatic movement of data between different types of storage devices. Tiering may occur within a single storage product or may span multiple storage products. For the purposes of ENERGY STAR certification, only tiering within a single storage product is applicable.

columns.name: Automated Storage Tiering Enabled in Hardware on Shipment
columns.description: If Intelligent Power Distribution Unit (iPDU) is enabled in hardware on shipment.

columns.name: Software Operating Environment Name
columns.description: Software operating environment/system name supported by storage configuration(s).

columns.name: Software Operating Environment Version
columns.description: Software operating environment/system version supported by storage configuration(s).

columns.name: Active or Passive Cooling Enabled in Hardware on Shipment
columns.description: If Active Cooling, Passive Cooling, or both is enabled in hardware on shipment.

columns.name: Length of Rolling Average for Input Power (s)
columns.description: If configuration(s) is/are capable of calculating an input power rolling average, the length of the rolling average is provided here.

columns.name: Length of Rolling Average for Inlet Air Temperature (s)
columns.description: If configuration(s) is/are capable of calculating an inlet air temperature, the length of the rolling average is provided here.

columns.name: Power and Temperature Reporting Additional Information
columns.description: If additional Power and Temperature Reporting information is available, it is provided here.

columns.name: UPC

The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: SNIA Online Taxonomy Category
columns.description: The SNIA Taxonomy is a categorization scheme for use in segmenting the data center storage market by end-use application and key product characteristics.  The three categories eligible for ENERGY STAR certification are Online 2, Online 3, and Online 4.

columns.name: Storage Controller Implementation
columns.description: A storage controller can be implemented in two different ways.  Scale-up storage is a storage product comprised of a discreet storage controller (with or without redundancy), which has a full view of all the storage devices in the storage product. Incremental storage capacity is added by the addition of storage devices under the control of the existing storage controller.  While Scale-out storage is a storage product capable of being comprised of two or more discreet storage controllers (with or without redundancy), combined with an overall integration or aggregation function resulting in a single storage product view for attached servers. Each discreet storage controller often has a partial view to a partition of the overall system's storage devices, but this is not a fixed requirement. Incremental storage capacity is added by the addition of storage devices under the control of the existing storage controller(s) and/or addition of additional storage devices along with additional controller(s).

columns.name: Storage Controller Advanced Data Recovery Type
columns.description: Advanced Data Recovery Capability is a collective term used in this specification to refer to error detection and correction features such as RAID, mirroring / grid technology, or other comparable advanced error detection and recovery systems.

columns.description: DATABASE measurement for storage device 2 optimal point. Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Storage Device 2 Raw Capacity (GB)
columns.description: The raw capacity of storage device 2 in GB.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Storage Device 1 Raw Capacity (GB)
columns.description: The raw capacity of storage device 1 in GB.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Storage Device 1 Type
columns.description: The type of storage device can be either a Hard Disk Drive (HDD) or a Solid State Drive (SSD).  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Optimal Point Ready Idle Workload Test (GB/W)
columns.description: The ready idle measurement for the highest predicted deployed storage device optimal point in GB per watt.

columns.name: Trans Auto Tiering Enabled Optimal Point
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Number of Redundant PSUs Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of redundant PSUs installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Num Redundant Controllers Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of redundant controllers installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Num of Controllers in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of controllers installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Trans Other Devices Used for Cache
columns.description: If Other was chosen for Trans Device Used for Cache, the type of Other device is identified here.

columns.name: Stream Other Devices Used for Cache
columns.description: If Other was chosen for Trans Device Used for Cache, the type of Other device is identified here.

columns.name: Stream Total Num Installed Storage Devices Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of storage devices installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Stream Ratio Devices Mixed Optimal Point
columns.description: The ratio of installed storage devices with addressable storage (not used for cache) during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.  If there is more than one device in the highest predicted deployed storage device, the ratio of each storage device is separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream Installed Solid State Devices in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of Solid State Devices installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.  If there is more than one device in the highest predicted deployed storage device, each device's number of solid state drives is separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream Installed Rotational Devices in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of rotational devices installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.  If there is more than one device in the highest predicted deployed storage device, each device's number of rotational drives is separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Stream Num of Controllers in Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of controllers installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Stream Num Redundant Controllers Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of redundant controllers installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Stream Number of PSUs Optimal Point
columns.description: The total number of PSUs installed during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Stream Auto Tiering Enabled Optimal Point
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the highest predicted deployed device(s) optimal point.

columns.name: Stream Storage Device 1 Form Factor
columns.description: The form factor of storage device 1 is either 2.5 inch, 3.5 inch, or Other.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Storage Device 1 Rated Speed (RPM)
columns.description: The rated speed of storage device 1 in RPM.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Num Devices Storage Device 1 Optimal Point
columns.description: The number of devices installed in storage device 1 optimal point.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Stream Physical or Modeled Data Device 1 Optimal Point
columns.description: The data for the storage device 1 optimal point is obtained by physically testing the system or through the use of modeled data.  Storage device 1 is the first device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Trans Devices Used for Cache
columns.description: The devices used for cache are identified here.  This can be DRAM, SSD, or Other device.

columns.name: Trans PSUs 80PLUS Certification
columns.description: The 80PLUS certification level achieved by the PSU(s) installed.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's certification level will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Trans PSU Rated Output (W)
columns.description: The Rated Output per installed PSU in watts.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's rated output will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: Trans PSU Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to the PSU(s) for sales and marketing purposes.  If there is more than one power supply, each power supply's model name will be separated by a semicolon.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certificationbodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performancecharacteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if itis recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string ofcharacters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assignedby EPA.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Comp Device 2 Optimal Point SW Build  Workload Test
columns.description: The SW Build measurement for the highest predicted deployed storage device optimal point

columns.name: Comp Automated Tiering Enabled During Testing Device 2
columns.description: If automated storage tiering (auto-tiering) was enabled during testing of the storage device 2.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Comp Num Devices Storage Device 2 Optimal Point
columns.description: The number of devices installed in storage device 2 optimal point.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Cap Storage Device 2 Raw Capacity (GB)
columns.description: The raw capacity of storage device 2 in GB.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

columns.name: Comp Storage Device 2 Form Factor
columns.description: The form factor of storage device 2 is either 2.5 inch, 3.5 inch, or Other.  Storage device 2 is the second device in addition to the highest predicted deployed storage device.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 2.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Data Center Storage that are effective as of March 15, 2021 or the Version 2.1 requirements that are effective as of January 19, 2022. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/office_equipment/data_center_storage/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Televisions

columns.name: Automatic Brightness Control
columns.description: The self-acting mechanism that controls the brightness of a TV as a function of ambient light.

columns.name: Features
columns.description: Lists the features available to the TV in general (not just a particular picture setting)

columns.name: Low Power Wireless Technologies Supported
columns.description: The wireless band and frequency supported by the model.

columns.name: Ethernet Supported
columns.description: Network connections, usually using Internet Protocol (IP).

columns.name: Resolution Format
columns.description: The resolution format is defined by the number of physical lines along the vertical and horizontal axis of the TV. A TV is High Definition (HD) if the resolution is 1920x1080 pixels, 4K if the resolution is 3840x2160 pixels, and 8K if the resolution is 7680x4320.

columns.name: Native Vertical Resolution (pixels)
columns.description: The physical pixel count for the vertical axis of the television (e.g., a television with a screen resolution of 1920 x 1080 (horizontal x vertical) would have a native vertical resolution of 1080).

columns.name: Backlight Technology Type
columns.description: The backlight technology used in the TV.

columns.name: Display Type
columns.description: The most common display types include Liquid Crystal Display (LCD) screens, that are either LED direct-lit or LED edge-lit displays, or organic light-emitting diode (OLED).

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description:
A Television is a product designed to produce dynamic video. A TV contains an internal TV tuner
encased within the product housing and is capable of receiving dynamic visual content from
wired or wireless sources. 

Also included are Home Theater Displays (HTDs), which have screen size greater than 25
inches and do not contain an internal TV tuner.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with uniqueperformance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and productspecification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certificationinformation from the certification body. Each row within the product list willhave a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track informationon certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Is Automatic Brightness Control Enabled by Default in the Default SDR Preset Picture Setting When Television is Shipped?
columns.description: This column tracks what is enabled by default in the Default SDR preset picture setting, not what is simply available to the TV.

columns.name: Average On Mode Power Consumption for Certification (watts)
columns.description: This is the calculated average of the On Mode Powers measured for the TV through CTA-2037C, not the Federal Test Procedure.

columns.name: Maximum Average On Mode Power for Certification (watts)
columns.description: This is the calculated average limit for the Average On Mode Power of the TV, which uses data from CTA-2037C, not the Federal Test Procedure.

columns.name: Reported On Mode Power (per the Federal Test Procedure) (watts)
columns.description: This value is obtained through the Federal Test Procedure during federal compliance testing and is reported during the ENERGY STAR certification process.

columns.name: Reported Annual Energy Consumption (kWh)
columns.description: This is a calculated value that uses data obtained through the AEC Test Method referenced in Section 4 (Testing) of the ENERGY STAR TVs Specification.

columns.name: Power Consumption in Standby Mode when Not Connected to a Network (watts)
columns.description: This is the measured Standby-Passive Mode Power as measured per the Standby-Passive Mode Test Method referenced in Section 4 (Testing) of the Version 9.0 TVs Specification.

columns.name: Power Consumption in Standby Mode when Connected to a Network (watts)
columns.description: This is the measured Standby-Active Mode Power as measured per the Standby-Active Mode Test Method referenced in Section 4 (Testing) of the Version 9.0 TVs Specification.

columns.name: Reported Standby-Active, Low Mode Power (watts)
columns.description: This is the Reported Standby-Active, Low Mode Power as measured per the Standby-Active, Low Mode Test Method referenced in Section 4 (Testing) of the Version 9.0 TVs Specification during federal compliance testing and is reported during the ENERGY STAR certification process.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Measured Standby Mode Power (Watts)
columns.description: This is the measured Standby Mode Power as measured per the Standby Mode Test Method referenced in Section 4 (Testing) of the ENERGY STAR TVs Specification.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Native Horizontal Resolution (pixels)
columns.description: The physical pixel count for the horizontal axis of the television (e.g., a television with a screen resolution of 1920 x 1080 (horizontal x vertical) would have a native horizontal resolution of 1920).

columns.name: Screen Area (sq. in.)
columns.description: The viewable screen area of the product, calculated by multiplying the viewable image width by the viewable image height.

columns.name: Diagonal Viewable Screen Size (in.)
columns.description: The diagonal screen size in inches.

columns.name: Application
columns.description: The market for which the product is intended.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 9.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Televisions that are effective as of October 20, 2022 or the Version 9.1 Program Requirements that are effective as of April 14, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/electronics/televisions/key_product_criteria.

This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: Options inlcude self priming pumps (for inground pools), non-self-priming pumps (for above ground pools), and pressure cleaner booster pumps (for additional pressure to operate certain pool cleaners).

columns.name: Motor Design
columns.description: Options include single-speed, multi-speed, and variable-speed (including variable-flow). Single speed pumps operate at one speed. Multi-speed pumps typically have two speeds and use low speed for filtration and high speed for cleaning. Variable speed pumps have continuously adjustable pump speeds and variable flow pumps have continuously adjustable output (flow rate). Variable Speed pumps deliver consistent flow, as flow restriction changes.

columns.name: Weighted Energy Factor (WEF) (kgal/kWh)
columns.description: Rated value for a measurement of pump efficiency (in kgal/kWh) based on performance at 1 or 2 DOE selected measurement points. For products with the ability to adjust speed while meeting flow needs, the WEF is able to account for energy savings from this adjustment.

columns.name: Field No Longer Used
columns.description: This field previously included information that was determined to no longer be relevant.

columns.name: Minimum WEF for Federal Standard
columns.description: The values here represent the minimum WEF the model is required to meet based on the configuration for minimum Federal Standards.

columns.name: Flow (GPM) at low speed measurement point
columns.description: The flow in GPM at the low speed measurement point for a product

columns.name: Head (ftH20) at low speed measurement point
columns.description: The head (vertical lift) supported by the pump at the low speed measurement point.

columns.name: RPM at low speed measurement point
columns.description: The rotation speed of the pump at the low speed measurement point.

columns.name: Power (W) at low speed measurement point
columns.description: The power measured at the low speed measurement point.

columns.name: Flow (GPM) at high speed measurement point
columns.description: The flow in GPM at the high speed measurement point for a product.

columns.name: RPM at high speed measurement point
columns.description: The rotation speed of the pump at the high speed measurement point.

columns.name: Head (ft.H20) at high speed point
columns.description: The head (vertical lift) supported by the pump at the high speed measurement point.

columns.name: Power (W) at high speed measurement point
columns.description: The power measured at the high speed measurement point.

columns.name: Speed (RPM)
columns.description: The speed in revolutions per minute (RPM) at Maximum pump speed. Curve A/B/C data is recorded at this speed.

columns.name: Curve-A Flow (GPM)
columns.description: The flow rate in gallons per minute (GPM) at which the Energy Factor was measured.

columns.name: Curve-A Power (W)
columns.description: The power in watts (W) at which the Energy Factor was measured.

columns.name: Curve-C Flow (GPM)
columns.description: The flow rate in gallons per minute (GPM) at which the Energy Factor was measured.

columns.name: Curve-B Flow (GPM)
columns.description: The flow rate in gallons per minute (GPM) at which the Energy Factor was measured.

columns.name: Curve-B Power (W)
columns.description: The power in watts (W) at which the Energy Factor was measured.

columns.name: Standby Average Power (W)
columns.description: The power draw when the pool pump is in Standby Power Mode. Only applicable to pool pumps with Standby Power Mode.

columns.name: Hydraulic Horsepower (HHP)
columns.description: A measurement  of the energy transferred to fluid flowing through the pump, calculated at max pump speed on Curve C, from the volume of fluid moved (gpm) and pressure (ftH2O).

columns.name: Rated Horsepower (HP)
columns.description: The motor power output (HP) designed by the manufacturer for rated revolutions per minute (RPM), voltage and frequency. May be less than Total Horsepower where the Service Factor is > 1.0, or equal to Total Horsepower where the Service Factor = 1.0. Also known as Nameplate Horsepower.

columns.name: Freeze Protection
columns.description: Is Pump equipped with freeze protection?

columns.name: Connected Capable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model meets optional connected criteria as defined in the specification, which may include functions helpful to the electrical grid (standardized signals and responses to manage energy use) and those helpful to consumers, such as scheduling and energy reporting.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: System can communicate using these protocols for sending messages and requests, some of which work wirelessly and some with wires (open standard media/network layer).

columns.name: DR Protocol
columns.description: Indicates which standardized protocol is used to send load management messages to the unit.

columns.name: Communication Hardware Architecture
columns.description: The Hardware Architecture describes the type of module/device within or external to the system needed for connection.

columns.name: Primary Communication Module/Device Brand Name
columns.description: An external communication module or device is required if communication hardware is not built into the unit.

columns.name: Primary Communication Module/Device Model Number
columns.description: An external communication module or device is required if communication hardware is not built into the unit.

columns.name: Features Enabled by Primary Communication Module/Device
Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 3.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements, effective on July 19, 2021, or Version 3.1 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements, also effective on July 19, 2021. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/other/pool_pumps/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Commercial Refrigerators and Freezers

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Defrost Type
columns.description: This includes automatic and manual defrost types.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Height (in.)
columns.description: The height of the unit measured in inches.

columns.name: Product Description
columns.description: This is the product type the model is marketed as. convertible temperature equipment, reach-in, roll-in, or pass- through units; merchandisers; under-counters; worktops; hybrid units; milk coolers; back bar coolers;  bottle coolers; deep well units; beer-dispensing or direct draw units; and bunker  freezers.  Refrigerated buffet tables and preparation tables, walk-in coolers, blast chillers and freezers, horizontal open equipment, vertical open equipment, semi-vertical open equipment, remote condensing equipment, ice cream freezer models are not eligible for ENERGY STAR.

columns.name: Brand Name

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner

columns.name: Total Display Area (sq. ft.)

columns.name: Type

columns.name: UPC

The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Model Information

Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Configuration (Vertical/Chest)
columns.description: The configuration of the cabinet through which access is gained (i.e., through a top-opening door, side-opening door or a slide opening drawer).

columns.name: Number of Transparent Doors
columns.description: For transparent and mix door models, the total number of transparent doors on the unit.

columns.name: Number of Solid Doors
columns.description: For solid and mix door models, the total number of solid doors on the unit.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date of which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Date Available On Market

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for commercial refrigerators and freezers include R-600a, R-290, R-450A, R-513A, and R-744 (CO₂).

columns.name: Reported Daily Energy Consumption (kWh/day)
columns.description: Provide the manufacturer reported Daily Energy Consumption values, which will appear on the ENERGY STAR list of qualified products. The directive (#2011-05) on Measured versus Reported Values for ENERGY STAR Certification addresses the instance where reported DECs may differ from measured DECs.

columns.name: Depth (in.)
columns.description: The depth of the unit measured in inches.

columns.name: Width (in.)
columns.description: The width of the unit measured in inches.

columns.name: Mixed Door Cabinet: Solid Door Volume (cu. ft.)
columns.description: For mixed door models, the volume of the solid door compartment measured in cubic feet.

columns.name: Mixed Door Cabinet: Transparent Door Volume (cu. ft.)
columns.description: For mixed door models, the volume of the transparent door compartment measured in cubic feet.

columns.name: Total Volume (cu. ft.)
columns.description: Total volume for all refrigerators (except service over counters), freezers, and hybrid models.

columns.name: Door Opening Orientation (Hinged/Sliding)
columns.description: The type of doors used to access the interior of the cabinet.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the refrigerant type used (e.g., R-134A, R-404A, CO2).

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Number of Drawers
columns.description: Number of drawers for chef base models.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 5.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Commercial Refrigerators and Freezers that are effective as of December 22, 2022. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/commercial_food_service_equipment/commercial_refrigerators_freezers/key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Water Coolers

columns.name: Hot-Water Dispenser Capacity (exact 6 oz. cups/hour)
columns.description: The amount of hot water a product can deliver at the required temperature, in number of exact 6 ounce cups per hour.

columns.name: On Mode Performance for Hot Water Draw
columns.description: A performance metric for efficiency of a product after hot water has been withdrawn. Higher OMP signifies higher efficiency.

columns.name: Daily Energy Use (On Mode With No Water Draw) (kWh/day)
columns.description: The required energy to maintain cold and/or hot water at appropriate dispensing temperatures for one day. This does not account for energy consumed after water has been withdrawn.

columns.name: Product Capacity Class
columns.description: "High Capacity coolers offer a greater volume of hot and cold water per hour, but use more energy. These products are appropriate for high-traffic spaces, like an office or commercial space.  Low Capacity water coolers offer greater energy savings, but produce less cold and hot water per hour. These products are a good choice for a home or other low-use application."

columns.name: Water Conditioning
columns.description: For conditioned storage water coolers, thermally conditioned water is stored in a tank in the water cooler and is available instantaneously. For on demand water coolers, water is heated as it is requested, which saves energy, but typically takes a few minutes to deliver.

columns.name: Water Source
columns.description: "The source of water for the water cooler.  Bottled: A bottle or reservoir supplies water to the water cooler. The bottle may be either top loading, or bottom loading (typically contained within the base of the unit). Point of Use Tap water is supplied to the water cooler through a pressurized water source. Conversion-type: The water cooler can be supplied by either a bottle or tap water."

columns.name: Type
columns.description: "Cold Only Units: These units dispense cold water only.  Cook and Cold Units: These units dispense both cold and room-temperature water.  Hot and Cold Units: These units dispense both hot and cold water.  Hot, Cook, and Cold Units: These units dispense hot, cold, and room temperature water."

columns.name: Date Qualified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Additional Product Capabilities
columns.description: Additional features may include connected capability, energy saving modes, or touchless dispensing.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the type of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: Refrigerant With GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP)

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: On Mode Performance for Cold Water Draw
columns.description: A performance metric for efficiency of a product after cold water has been withdrawn. Higher OMP signifies higher efficiency.

columns.name: Dispensed Water Temperature - Cold Water (F)
columns.description: Water temperature from the on mode with no water draw portion of the test.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Additional Dispensing Capabilites
columns.description: Some ENERGY STAR water coolers offer sparkling, flavored, or alkaline water in addition to cold water.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Cold-Water Dispenser Capacity (gal/hour)
columns.description: The amount of cold water a product can deliver at the required temperature, in gallons per hour.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 3.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Water Coolers that are effective March 23, 2022 . A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/water_coolers .

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Residential Dishwashers

columns.name: US Federal Standard (gallons/cycle)
columns.description: The mandatory water efficiency standards for residential dishwashers, established by DOE rulemaking activities and U.S. federal laws.

columns.name: Soil-Sensing Capability
columns.description: A soil-sensing dishwasher  means a dishwasher that has the ability to adjust any energy-consuming aspect of the normal cycle based on the soil load of the dishes.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: Communication Hardware Architecture
columns.description: The Hardware Architecture describes the type of module/device within or external to the system needed for connection.

columns.name: US Federal Standard (kWh/yr)
columns.description: The mandatory energy standards for residential dishwashers, established by DOE rulemaking activities and U.S. federal laws.

columns.name: % Better than US Federal Standard (kWh/yr)
columns.description: How much less energy the unit uses per year relative to the U.S. Federal standard, expressed as a percent.

columns.name: Additional Product Features
columns.description: Features applicable to the dishwasher may include hard food filter, self-cleaning filter, cycle status lights, 3rd rack, top controls, and/or front controls.

columns.name: % Better than US Federal Standard (gallons/cycle)
columns.description: How much less water the unit uses per cycle relative to the U.S. Federal standard, expressed as a percent.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Direct on-premises Open-standard Based Interconnection
columns.description: Connected appliances may or may not enable open-standards connectivity in the home. Consider the following illustrative examples: "Yes" An appliance that can directly communicate using Wi-Fi and OpenADR, or wired Ethernet and SEP 2.x; "No" An appliance that uses proprietary communications to/from the cloud, and is capable of supporting open-standards communications only in the cloud.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Water Use (gallons/cycle)
columns.description: This is the estimated per cycle water use of the dishwasher under typical conditions, expressed as the number of gallons of water delivered to the machine during one cycle, as measured by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Subpart B, Section 430, Appendix C. Actual water consumption will vary depending on the settings chosen.

columns.name: DR Protocol
columns.description: Indicates which standardized protocol is used to send load management messages to the unit.

columns.name: Depth (inches)
columns.description: Product depth when the door is shut and not including the handle.

columns.name: Tub Material
columns.description: Tub material(s) could be stainless steel, plastic, hybrid, or other.

columns.name: Drying Method
columns.description: Drying method(s) applicable to the dishwasher may include air dry, condensation dry, fan-assisted dry, heated dry, mineral dry, and/or automatic door release.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Width (inches)
columns.description: Product width.

columns.name: Capacity - Maximum Number of Place Settings
columns.description: The maximum number of place settings as recommended by the manufacturer.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Annual Energy Use (kWh/yr)
columns.description: This is the estimated annual energy use of the dishwasher under typical conditions, including the machine energy used by the dishwasher during a dishwashing cycle, any additional energy used by a water heater to heat the water, and the energy used by the dishwasher while in standby mode. It is based on an annual usage of 215 loads per year, or around 4 per week, as referenced by the U.S. Department of Energy test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Subpart B, Appendix C. Actual energy consumption will vary depending on your usage patterns, including how often you run the dishwasher and what cycle you select.

columns.name: Connected Capable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model meets optional connected criteria as defined in the specification, which may include functions helpful to the electrical grid (standardized signals and responses to manage energy use) and those helpful to consumers, such as scheduling and energy reporting.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what you need in your home to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control, e.g. ‘WiFi’ or Zigbee.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: Type refers to whether the product is considered a standard-sized dishwasher or a compact-sized dishwasher. Standard-sized models can fit at least eight place settings and six serving pieces. Products that cannot fit those amounts are considered compact-sized models.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 7.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Residential Dishwashers that are effective as of July 19, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/dishwashers/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Room Air Conditioners

columns.name: Depth (inches)
columns.description: The depth of the product in inches.

columns.name: Network Standby Power (watts)
columns.description: Average power consumption of the unit when in automatic sleep mode.

columns.name: Combined Energy Efficiency Ratio (CEER)
columns.description: Combined Energy Efficiency Ratio (CEER) is an energy performance metric for room air conditioners, as measured by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Appendix F. The higher the CEER, the more efficient the room air conditioner. The metric has units Btu/Wh, and is calculated as the quotient of the cooling capacity divided by the average rate of electrical energy consumed by the air conditioner during operational and non-operational modes.

columns.name: Casement Window
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR room air conditioner criteria covers both the casement-only and casement-slider product classes. Casement-only refers to a room air conditioner designed for mounting in a casement window. Casement-slider refers to a room air conditioner with an encased assembly designed for mounting in a sliding or casement window.

columns.name: Height (inches)
columns.description: The height of the product in inches.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: Room air conditioners with louvered sides are designed for mounting in a window, while units without louvered sides (also commonly referred to as "built-in") are designed for mounting through the wall of a building.

columns.name: Communication Hardware Architecture
columns.description: The Hardware Architecture describes the type of module/device within or external to the system needed for connection.

columns.name: DR Protocol
columns.description: Indicates which standardized protocol is used to send load management messages to the unit.

columns.name: Variable Speed Compressor
columns.description: Room air conditioners with variable speed compressors are able to save a significant amount of energy during operation by continuously and quietly regulating the temperature. Room air conditioners with conventional compressors are either operating at maximum capacity when the thermostat calls for cooling or off when the desired temperature has been achieved and typically alternating frequently between on at maximum capacity and off.

columns.name: Support Bracket
columns.description: Some room air conditioners require a support bracket on the outside of the windowsill for installation.

columns.name: Connected Capable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model offers advanced controls and increased functionality such as the ability to turn off the unit remotely when not at home and feedback on the energy use of the product. Room air conditioners with connected functionality are smart grid ready; if linked with your energy provider or an energy management system these products can dynamically adjust their energy use, potentially lowering your electric bill.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the type of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Network Security Standards
columns.description: Describes the standards implemented in the product that provide privacy best practices and additional security by preventing or detecting network compromises.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Cooling Capacity (Btu/hr)
columns.description: The cooling capacity of the room air conditioner, in Btu (British Thermal Units) per hour, as measured by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Appendix F. A higher Btu/hr means the product offers a greater cooling capability.

columns.name: Percent Less Energy Use than US Federal Standard
columns.description: This percent indicates how much each model exceeds the U.S. federal standard for its product class.

columns.name: Weight (lbs)
columns.description: The weight of the room air conditioner, not including packaging, in lbs (pounds).

columns.name: Width (inches)
columns.description: The width of the product in inches.

columns.name: Installation Mounting Type
columns.description: Most room air conditioners are installed on a windowsill. Some room air conditioners will straddle the window or straddle the windowsill.

columns.name: Voltage (volts)
columns.description: The rated voltage of the room air conditioner, in volts.

columns.name: Primary Communication Module/Device Brand Name and Model Number
columns.description: An external communication module or device is required if communication hardware is not built into the unit.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what you need in your home to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control, e.g. ‘WiFi’ or Zigbee.

columns.name: Heating Mode
columns.description: Refers to the heating function found in certain room air conditioner models.

columns.name: Is Broadband Internet Connection Needed for Demand Response?
columns.description: Describes whether the product can participate in utility demand response programs without the home having a broadband connection.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Product Class
columns.description: Classification of products by the type of energy used or by capacity or other performance-related features that affect efficiency. Different ENERGY STAR levels may apply to different product classes.

columns.name: Annual Energy Use (kWh/yr)
columns.description: This number is the estimated annual energy use in kilowatt hours per year of the room air conditioner under typical conditions. This figure assumes 750 hours of compressor operation per year but will vary depending on the indoor and outdoor temperature and hours of use.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: Direct on-premises Open-standard Based Interconnection
columns.description: Connected appliances may or may not enable open-standards connectivity in the home. Consider the following illustrative examples: "Yes" An appliance that can directly communicate using Wi-Fi and OpenADR, or wired Ethernet and SEP 2.x; "No" An appliance that uses proprietary communications to/from the cloud, and is capable of supporting open-standards communications only in the cloud.

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for room air conditioners include R-32, R-454B, R-290, R-600a, R-441A, R-452B, R-454A, R-454C, and R-457A.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 5.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Room Air Conditioners that are effective as of October 30, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/heating_cooling/air_conditioning_room/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment - AC-Output

columns.name: Dual Input Level 2: No Vehicle Mode Power Factor
columns.description: This indicates the No Vehicle Mode Power Factor for the Level 2 option of a dual input model. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average No Vehicle Mode Power Factor for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This indicates for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Maximum Output Power (kW)
columns.description: This indicates the maximum output power in kW.

columns.name: Dual Input No Vehicle Mode Total Allowance (watts)
columns.description: The No Vehicle Mode Total Allowance is the maximum amount of power allowed in this mode for the model to be ENERGY STAR certified for the Level 2 option of a dual input model.

columns.name: Dual Input Level 2: 15 A Operation Mode Test: Total Loss (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the 15 A Operation Mode Total Loss for the Level 2 option of a dual input model. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Full Current Operation Mode Loss for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Partial On Mode Total Allowance (watts)
columns.description: The Partial On Mode Total Allowance is the maximum amount of power allowed in this mode for the model to be ENERGY STAR certified.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Idle Mode Power Factor
columns.description: This indicates the Idle Mode Power Factor for all models and for those with ABC enabled. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Idle Mode Power Factor for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Full Current Operation Mode Test: Total Loss (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the Full Current Operation Mode Total Loss for all models and for those with ABC enabled. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Full Current Operation Mode Loss for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: 15 A Operation Mode Test: Total Loss (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the 15 A Operation Mode Total Loss for all models and for those with ABC enabled. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Full Current Operation Mode Loss for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Dual Input Level 2: Partial On Mode Input Power (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the Partial On Mode Input Power for the Level 2 option of a dual input model. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Input Power for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Dual Input Partial On Mode Total Allowance (watts)
columns.description: The Partial On Mode Total Allowance is the maximum amount of power allowed in this mode for the model to be ENERGY STAR certified for the Level 2 option of a dual input model.

columns.name: Dual Input Level 2:  Idle Mode Input Power (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the Idle Mode Input Power for the Level 2 option of a dual input model. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Input Power for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Dual Input Idle Mode Total Allowance (watts)

columns.description: This indicates the Idle Mode Power Factor for the Level 2 option of a dual input model. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Idle Mode Power Factor for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Date Certified
