columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Dual Input Level 2: 30 A Operation Mode Test: Total Loss (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the 30 A Operation Mode Total Loss for the Level 2 option of a dual input model. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Full Current Operation Mode Loss for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Dual Input Level 2: Full Current Operation Mode Test: Total Loss (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the Full Current Operation Mode Total Loss for the Level 2 option of a dual input model. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Full Current Operation Mode Loss for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Dual Input Level 2:  Partial On Mode Power Factor
columns.description: This indicates the Partial On Mode Power Factor for the Level 2 option of a dual input model. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Partial On Mode Power Factor for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Dual Input Level 2: No Vehicle Mode Input Power (watts)

columns.name: 4 A Operation Mode Test: Total Loss (watts)

columns.name: 30 A Operation Mode Test: Total Loss (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the 30 A Operation Mode Total Loss for all models and for those with ABC enabled. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Full Current Operation Mode Loss for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Idle Mode Total Allowance (watts)
columns.description: The Idle Mode Total Allowance is the maximum amount of power allowed in this mode for the model to be ENERGY STAR certified.

columns.name: Idle Mode Input Power (watts)

columns.name: Partial On Mode Power Factor

columns.name: Partial On Mode Input Power (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the Partial On Mode Input Power for all models and for those with ABC enabled. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Input Power for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: No Vehicle Mode Power Factor
columns.description: This indicates the No Vehicle Mode Power Factor for all models and for those with ABC enabled. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average No Vehicle Mode Power Factor for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: No Vehicle Mode Total Allowance (watts)
columns.description: The No Vehicle Mode Total Allowance is the maximum amount of power allowed in this mode for the model to be ENERGY STAR certified.

columns.name: No Vehicle Mode Input Power (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the No Vehicle Mode Input Power for all models and for those with ABC enabled. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Input Power for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Network Connection Types Available
columns.description: This indicates which, if any, network protocols that the model is shipped with, and None if the model does not have any network connected capability.

columns.name: Network Capability
columns.description: Indicates whether the model is able to connect to a network.

columns.name: Product Features
columns.description: This indicates other features of this EVSE (e.g., vehicle to grid capability, Smart Home Energy Management System (SHEMS) compatibility, etc.).

columns.name: Is Broadband Internet Connection Needed for Demand Response?
columns.description: Describes whether the product can participate in utility demand response programs without the home having a broadband connection.

columns.name: Network Security Standards
columns.description: Describes the standards implemented in the product that provide privacy best practices and additional security by preventing or detecting network compromises.

columns.name: Protocols Used to Support Smart Charging
columns.description: Indicates if the model has the transceiver and/or necessary hardware to support smart charging (i.e. beyond simple managed charging with pulse width modulation or CANBus) using any of these station-to-vehicle protocols.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: System can communicate using these protocols for sending messages and requests, some of which work wirelessly and some with wires (communication standard media/network layer).

columns.name: DR Protocol
columns.description: Indicates which standardized protocol is used to send load management messages to the unit.

columns.name: Connected Capable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model meets optional connected criteria as defined in the specification, which may include functions helpful to the electrical grid (standardized signals and responses to manage energy use) and those helpful to consumers, such as scheduling and energy reporting.

columns.name: Automatic Brightness Control (ABC) Capable?
columns.description: This indicates if this model is capable of the self-acting mechanism that controls the brightness of a display or lamp as a function of ambient light.

columns.name: Maximum (100%) Measured Luminance of the High Res Display (candelas per m2)
columns.description: Maximum luminance of the display, if EVSE has a high resolution display measured at maximum available brightness.

columns.name: Screen Area, if EVSE has high res display (in2)
columns.description: The screen area, if the EVSE has a high resolution display.

columns.name: Output Cord Gauge (AWG)
columns.description: The cord set American Wire Gauge (AWG) used for test or mean cord set AWG if multiple-output. If no cord was provided for testing, then there will be a NA.

columns.name: Maximum Output Cord Length (ft.)
columns.description: This indicates cord set length or mean cord set length, if multiple-output. If 0, there is no cord provided with the model during testing.

columns.name: Number of Outputs
columns.description: This indicates the number of outputs available for this model.

columns.name: Input Voltage (V)
columns.description: This indicates the input voltage.

columns.name: Max Nameplate Output Current (A)
columns.description: This indicates the maximum nameplate current in Amperes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. For light commercial HVAC models, model number indicates the outdoor model number.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: This indicates which type of EVSE describes this model.

columns.name: Auxiliary Product Features
columns.description: Indicates if the model has the auxiliary features to meet regulatory requirements such as credit card readers, revenue grade meters, ISO 15118 PLC boards, RFID and those helpful to consumers such as speakers, display, cameras and occupancy sensing.

columns.name: Dual Input Level 2: 4 A Operation Mode Test: Total Loss (watts)
columns.description: This indicates the 4 A Operation Mode Total Loss for the Level 2 option of a dual input model. For those with ABC enabled, this is the average Full Current Operation Mode Loss for the high and low illuminance conditions.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements for AC-output models as listed in the Version 1.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment that are effective as of December 27, 2016, the Version 1.1 requirements effective as of March 31, 2021, or the Version 1.2 requirements effective as of June 20, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/evse_key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Commercial Ovens

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description:
A unique string of characters assigned by certification
bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance
characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it
is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of
characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned
by EPA.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Baking Production Capacity (lbs/hr)
columns.description: The baking-energy efficiency production capacity of a rack oven operating in baking mode is expressed in pounds per hour. The production rate is the average rate at which the oven brings a specified food product to a specified final temperature.

columns.name: Rack Oven Baking-Energy Efficiency (%)
columns.description: The baking-energy efficiency percentage for a rack oven operating in baking mode is the quantity of energy input to the food product (i.e., frozen pies) during the baking process; expressed as a percentage of the quantity of energy input to the oven during the cooking-energy efficiency tests.

columns.name: Convection Mode Production Capacity (lbs/hr)
columns.description: The cooking-energy efficiency production capacity of a convection oven or a combination oven operating in convection mode is expressed in pounds per hour. The production rate is the average rate at which the oven brings a specified food product to a specified final temperature.

columns.name: Steam Mode Cooking Energy Efficiency (%)
columns.description: The cooking-energy efficiency percentage for a combination oven operating in steam mode is the quantity of energy input to the food product (i.e., potatoes) during the cooking process; expressed as a percentage of the quantity of energy input to the oven during the cooking-energy efficiency tests.

columns.name: Convection Mode Cooking Energy Efficiency (%)
columns.description: The cooking-energy efficiency percentage for an oven operating in convection mode is the quantity of energy input to the food product (i.e., potatoes) during the cooking process; expressed as a percentage of the quantity of energy input to the oven during the cooking-energy efficiency tests.

columns.name: Gas Oven Steam Mode Total Idle Energy Rate (Btu/hr)
columns.description: The average rate of energy consumed in Btu/h by the gas  combination oven operating in steam mode while idling at a ready-to-cook temperature set point.

columns.name: Half-Size and Full-Size Steam Pan Capacity
columns.description: The number of full-size (GN 1/1) steam pans that a half- or full-size combination oven can accommodate.

columns.name: Steam Mode Pre-Heat Time (min)
columns.description: The measured amount of time an oven requires to pre-heat in steam mode.

columns.name: Gas Oven Steam Mode Pre-Heat Energy (Btu)
columns.description: The measured amount of energy a gas oven in steam mode consumes during the pre-heat test.

columns.name: Convection Mode Pre-Heat Time (min)
columns.description: The measured amount of time an oven requires to pre-heat in convection mode.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. This column includes alternative means to identify the model/model family (e.g., additional model names, additional model numbers, retailer SKUs, product descriptions).

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Half-Size Sheet Pan Capacity
columns.description: The number of half-size sheet pans a half-size convection oven can accommodate.

columns.name: Gas Oven Convection Mode Electric Idle Energy Rate (kW)
columns.description: The average rate of auxillary electric energy consumed (i.e., fan motor, lighting, controls, etc.) in Watts by the gas convection oven or combination oven operating in convection mode while idling at a ready-to-cook temperature set point.

columns.name: Electric Oven Convection Mode Pre-Heat Energy (kWh)
columns.description: The measured amount of energy an electric oven in convection mode consumes during the pre-heat test.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Steam Injection Period Water Consumption (gal/min)
columns.description: The average water consumption rate of a convection or rack oven operating in steam injection mode during steam injection periods expressed in gallons per minute.

columns.name: Set-Back Idle Mode
columns.description: The rack oven has a default setback mode during extended periods of non-use where the oven is idling at a reduced temperature set point.

columns.name: Steam Mode Idle Period Water Consumption (gal/hr/pan)
columns.description: The average water consumption rate of a combination oven operating in steam mode during idle periods expressed in gallons per hour per pan.

columns.name: Steam Mode Cooking Period Water Consumption (gal/pan)
columns.description: The average water consumption rate of a combination oven operating in steam mode during cooking periods expressed in gallons per pan.

columns.name: Convection Mode Idle Period Water Consumption (gal/hr/pan)
columns.description: The average water consumption rate of a combination oven operating in convection mode during idle periods expressed in gallons per hour per pan.

columns.name: Convection Mode Cooking Period Water Consumption (gal/pan)
columns.description: The average water consumption rate of a combination oven operating in convection mode during cooking periods expressed in gallons per pan.

columns.name: Steam Mode Production Capacity (lbs/hr)
columns.description: The cooking-energy efficiency production capacity of a combination oven operating in steam mode is expressed in pounds per hour. The production rate is the average rate at which the oven brings a specified food product to a specified final temperature.

columns.name: Gas Oven Steam Mode Electric Idle Energy Rate (kW)
columns.description: The average rate of auxillary electric energy consumed (i.e., fan motor, lighting, controls, etc.) in Watts by the gas combination oven operating in steam mode while idling at a ready-to-cook temperature set point.

columns.name: Gas Oven Steam Mode Idle Energy Rate (Btu/hr)
columns.description: The average rate of primary energy consumed by gas ovens (i.e., gas is primary heat source for gas ovens).

columns.name: Electric Oven Steam Mode Idle Energy Rate (kW)
columns.description: The average rate of energy consumed in kW by the electric combination oven operating in steam mode while idling at a ready-to-cook temperature set point.

columns.name: Full-Size Sheet Pan Capacity
columns.description: The number of  full-size sheet pans a full-size convection oven can accommodate.

columns.name: Method of Steam Generation
columns.description: The approach used for all ovens to generate steam while operating in steam mode (e.g. boiler/steam generator, boilerless, or spritzer/injector).

columns.name: Gas Oven Convection Mode Total Idle Energy Rate (Btu/hr)
columns.description: For ovens with variable Btu/h input, each available input shall be tested individually and meet the cooking energy efficiency and idle energy rate requirements of the specification. The calculated gas or electric oven convection mode idle energy rate value.

columns.name: Gas Oven Convection Mode Idle Energy Rate (Btu/hr)
columns.description: The average rate of primary energy consumed by gas ovens (i.e., gas is primary heat source for gas ovens).

columns.name: Two-Thirds-Size Steam Pan Capacity
columns.description: The number of two-thirds size (GN 2/3) steam pans that a two-thirds size combination oven can accommodate.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: Designates type of commercial oven model in simple terms as one of the following: electric convection oven, gas convection oven, electric combination oven, gas combination oven, gas single rack oven, or gas double rack oven.

columns.name: Oven Classification
columns.description: Designates the type of commercial oven model per ASTM F1495-20 specification, as either Type I (table, countertop, and stand mounted units) or Type II (floor and roll-in units).

columns.name: Size
columns.description: Designates the size of the commercial convection and combination oven model (but not gas rack ovens), according to the following options for Version 3.0: 1) Full-size convection oven (a convection oven that is able to accomodate standard full-size sheet pans measuring 18 x 26 x 1-inch); 2) Half-size convection oven (a convection oven that is able to accomodate half-sheet pans measuring 18 x 13 x 1-inch); 3) Full-size combination ovens (a combination oven that is able to accomodate two GN 1/1 steam pans measuring 20 3/4 x 12 3/4-inch, per rack or tray position); 4) Half-size combination ovens (a combination oven that is able to accomodate a one GN 1/1 steam pan measuring 20 3/4 x 12 3/4-inch, per rack or tray position); and two-third-size combination ovens (a combination oven that is able to accomodate one GN 2/3 steam pan measuring 13 3/4 x 12 3/4-inch, per rack or tray position). The Version 3.0 specification covers full-size and half-size electric convection ovens; full-size gas convection ovens; full- and half-size electric and gas combination ovens; two-third-size combination ovens.

columns.name: Heat Source Fuel Type
columns.description: Gas or electric fuel type.

columns.name: Input Rate (kW)
columns.description: For electric ovens with variable kW input, each available input shall be tested individually and meet the cooking energy efficiency and idle energy rate requirements of the specification.

columns.name: Input Rate (Btu/hr)
columns.description: For gas ovens with variable Btu/h input, each available input shall be tested individually and meet the cooking energy efficiency and idle energy rate requirements of the specification.

columns.name: Gas Oven Convection Mode Pre-Heat Energy (Btu)
columns.description: The measured amount of energy a gas oven in convection mode consumes during the pre-heat test.

columns.name: Electric Oven Steam Mode Pre-Heat Energy (kWh)
columns.description: The measured amount of energy an electric oven in steam mode consumes during the pre-heat test.

columns.name: Electric Oven Convection Mode Idle Energy Rate (kW)
columns.description: For ovens with variable kW input, each available input shall be tested individually and meet the cooking energy efficiency and idle energy rate requirements of the specification. The calculated gas or electric oven convection mode idle energy rate value.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the V3.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Commercial Ovens that are effective as of January 12, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/commercial_food_service_equipment/commercial_ovens/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment - DC-Output

columns.name: Average Loading-Adjusted Efficiency (%) AC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the calculated average loading-adjusted charging efficiency of the model at various loading and temperature conditions, according to Equations 6 and 7 of the specification.   The ENERGY STAR Version 1.1 specification only has Operation Mode (active charging) criteria for DC EVSE up to and including 65 kW. Models between 65 kW and 350 kW must be tested in Operation Mode and report the results but there are no current criteria for these models in this mode.

columns.name: Maximum Nameplate Output Current (A) DC- Input
columns.description: Indicates the maximum nameplate output current for an DC-input EVSE.

columns.name: Rated Input Voltage (V) DC-Input
columns.description: Indicates the rated input voltage for an DC-input EVSE.

columns.name: No Vehicle Mode Input Power (watts) DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the No Vehicle Mode Input Power for DC- Input EVSE. If the model has a high resolution display with ABC enabled, this is the average Input Power for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Partial On Mode Input Power (watts) DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Partial On Mode Input Power for DC-input EVSE. If the model has a high resolution display with ABC enabled, this is the average Input Power for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Idle Mode Input Power (watts) DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Idle Mode Input Power for DC-Input EVSE. If the model has a high resolution display with ABC enabled, this is the average Input Power for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 25% Loading Condition Input Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 25% of the maximum available output power in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 25% Loading Condition Output Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 25% of the maximum available output power in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 50% Loading Condition Input Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 50% of the maximum available output power in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 50% Loading Condition Output Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 50% of the maximum available output power in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 75% Loading Condition Input Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 75% of the maximum available output power in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 50 kW Loading Condition Input Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 50 kW in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 50 kW Loading Condition Output Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 50 kW in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 150 kW Loading Condition Input Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 150 kW in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 150 kW Loading Condition Output Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 150 kW in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 100% Loading Condition Input Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 100% of the maximum available output power in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 100% Loading Condition Output Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 100% of the maximum available output power in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 25% Loading Condition Input Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 25% of the maximum available output power in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 25% Loading Condition Output Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 25% of the maximum available output power in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 50% Loading Condition Input Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 50% of the maximum available output power in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 50% Loading Condition Output Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 50% of the maximum available output power in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 75% Loading Condition Input Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 75% of the maximum available output power in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 75% Loading Condition Output Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 75% of the maximum available output power in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 50 kW Loading Condition Input Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 50 kW in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 50 kW Loading Condition Output Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 50 kW in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Maximum Nameplate Output Current (A) AC-Input
columns.description: Indicates the maximum nameplate output current for an AC-input EVSE.

columns.name: Number of Outputs
columns.description: Indicates the number of outputs available for this model.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 100% Loading Condition Input Power in Cold Climate AC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during active charging) at 100% of the maximum available output power in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Input Power for an AC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 100% Loading Condition Input Power in Hot Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 100% of the maximum available output power in the hot climate condition of 104° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 100% Loading Condition Output Power in Hot Climate AC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during active charging) at 100% of the maximum available output power in the hot climate condition of 104° F. The Output Power for an AC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 150 kW Loading Condition Input Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 150 kW in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 75% Loading Condition Output Power in Cold Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 75% of the maximum available output power in the cold climate condition of 20° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Network Security Standards
columns.description: Describes the standards implemented in the product that provide privacy best practices and additional security by preventing or detecting network compromises.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This indicates for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, UPC codes, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: DC-input or AC-input
columns.description: This indicates if the EVSE is intended to receive AC- or DC-input power, or if it is capable of accepting both.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 150 kW Loading Condition Output Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 150 kW in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 100% Loading Condition Input Power in Temperate Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 100% of the maximum available output power in the temperate climate condition of 68° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 25% Loading Condition Input Power in Hot Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 25% of the maximum available output power in the hot climate condition of 104° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 25% Loading Condition Output Power in Hot Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Output Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 25% of the maximum available output power in the hot climate condition of 104° F. The Output Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Operation Mode: 50% Loading Condition Input Power in Hot Climate DC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Input Power of the model when tested in Operation Mode (during Active charging) at 50% of the maximum available output power in the hot climate condition of 104° F. The Input Power for a DC-input is listed.

columns.name: Single Phase or Three Phase
columns.description: This indicates if the EVSE is single-phase, three-phase, or both.

columns.name: Product Configuration
columns.description: This indicates whether the model is a Distributed Product Configuration (a DC-output EVSE that has its functional components distributed between more than one separate enclosures) or an All-in-One Configuration (a DC-output EVSE that has all of its components in one enclosure).

columns.name: Connector Type
columns.description: This indicates the type(s) of connector shipped with this product: combined charging system (CCS), CHAdeMO, Tesla, or other)

columns.name: Network Capability
columns.description: Indicates whether the model is able to connect to a network.

columns.name: Network Connection Types Available
columns.description: This indicates which, if any, network protocols that the model is shipped with, and None if the model does not have any network connected capability.

columns.name: Output Cord Length (ft.)
columns.description: This indicates cord set length or mean cord set length, if multiple-output. If 0, there is no cord provided with the model during testing.

columns.name: Output Cord Gauge (AWG)
columns.description: The cord set American Wire Gauge (AWG) used for test or mean cord set AWG if multiple-output. If no cord was provided for testing, then there will be a NA.

columns.name: Screen Area, if EVSE has high res display (in2)
columns.description: The screen area, if the EVSE has a high resolution display.

columns.name: Maximum Measured Luminance of the High Res Display (candelas per m2)
columns.description: Maximum luminance of the display, if EVSE has a high resolution display measured at maximum available brightness.

columns.name: Automatic Brightness Control Capable?
columns.description: This indicates if this model is capable of the self-acting mechanism that controls the brightness of a display or lamp as a function of ambient light.

columns.name: DR Protocol
columns.description: Indicates which standardized protocol is used to send load management messages to the unit.

columns.name: Protocols Used to Support Smart Charging
columns.description: Indicates if the model has the transceiver and/or necessary hardware to support smart charging (i.e. beyond simple managed charging with pulse width modulation or CANBus) using any of these station-to-vehicle protocols.

columns.name: Is Broadband Internet Connection Needed for Demand Response?
columns.description: Describes whether the product can participate in utility demand response programs without the home having a broadband connection.

columns.name: Auxiliary Product Features
columns.description: Indicates if the model has the auxiliary features to meet regulatory requirements such as credit card readers, revenue grade meters, ISO 15118 PLC boards, RFID and those helpful to consumers such as speakers, display, cameras and occupancy sensing.

columns.name: Product Features
columns.description: This indicates other features of this EVSE (e.g., vehicle to grid capability, Smart Home Energy Management System (SHEMS) compatibility, etc.).

columns.name: Rated Input Voltage (V) AC-Input
columns.description: Indicates the rated input voltage for an AC-input EVSE.

columns.name: Integral Battery Bank
columns.description: Indicates if the EVSE has an integral, internal battery bank.

columns.name: No Vehicle Mode Total Allowance (watts)
columns.description: The No Vehicle Mode Total Allowance is the maximum amount of power allowed in this mode for the model to be ENERGY STAR certified.

columns.name: No Vehicle Mode Power Factor AC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the No Vehicle Mode Power Factor for all AC-input models. If the model has a high resolution display with ABC enabled, this is the average No Vehicle Mode Power Factor for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Partial On Mode Total Allowance (watts)
columns.description: The Partial On Mode Total Allowance is the maximum amount of power allowed in this mode for the model to be ENERGY STAR certified.

columns.name: Partial On Mode Power Factor AC-Input
columns.description: This indicates the Partial On Mode Power Factor for all AC-input models. If the model has a high resolution display with ABC enabled, this is the average Partial On Mode Power Factor for the high and low illuminance conditions.

columns.name: Average Loading-Adjusted Efficiency (%) DC-Input
columns.description:
This indicates the calculated average loading-adjusted charging efficiency of the model at various loading and temperature conditions, according to Equations 6 and 7 of the specification. 

The ENERGY STAR Version 1.1 specification only has Operation Mode (Active charging) criteria for DC EVSE up to and including 65 kW. Models between 65 kW and 350 kW must be tested in Operation Mode and report the results but there are no current criteria for these models in this mode.

columns.description: This indicates the No Vehicle Mode Input Power for AC- Input EVSE. If the model has a high resolution display with ABC enabled, this is the average Input Power for the high and low illuminance conditions.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements for DC-output models as listed in the Version 1.1 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment that are effective as of March 31, 2021 or the V1.2 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements that are effective as of June 20, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/evse_key_product_criteria.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Residential Electric Cooking Products

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Low-Power Mode Energy Consumption for Oven Component (kWh/year)
columns.description: The annual combined low-power mode energy consumption of the conventional electric oven component of a combined electric cooking product, ETLP,O (kWh/year) based on measured test data.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #3 Wattage (watts)
columns.description: The wattage of the third cooking zone.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #4 Wattage (watts)
columns.description: The wattage of the fourth cooking zone.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Product Features
columns.description: Indicates additional product features of the cooking top such as sensors, indicator lights, control locks, or active cooling.

columns.name: Control Type
columns.description: Indicates the control interface type for turning on/off cooking top. May include digital, knobs, or touch controls.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #6 Size (inches)
columns.description: The maximum size of the sixth cooking zone.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #5 Size (inches)
columns.description: The maximum size of the fifth cooking zone.

columns.name: Height (inches)
columns.description: The product height (inches) excluding the handles or knobs.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Cooking Top Technology
columns.description: Designates whether the cooktop technology is induction, radiant, coil, or something else.

columns.name: Specialty Zones
columns.description: Indicates available specialty zones on the cooking top, such as, simmering zone, warming zone, melting zone, griddle zone, power heating zone, concentric cooking zones, bridge element, and others.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Width (inches)
columns.description: The product width (inches) excluding the handles or knobs.

columns.name: Annual Energy Consumption (kWh/year)
columns.description: The rated/reported Annual Energy Consumption. This is the estimated annual energy use of the cooking top under typical conditions, including the active mode and the energy used by the cooking top while in low-power mode. It is based on an annual usage of 418 cooking events (averaging 31 minutes each) per year, or around 8 per week, as referenced by the U.S. Department of Energy test procedure, Code of Federal Regulations, Title 10, Section 430, Subpart B, Appendix I1. Actual energy consumption will vary depending on your usage patterns, including how often you use the cooking top.

columns.name: Average Cooking Zone Time (minutes)
columns.description: Average heat up time to 90C of all cooking zones.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #3 Size (inches)
columns.description: The maximum size of the third cooking zone.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #1 Size (inches)
columns.description: The maximum size of the first cooking zone.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #4 Size (inches)
columns.description: The maximum size of the fourth cooking zone.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #2 Size (inches)
columns.description: The maximum size of the second cooking zone.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Smart Capabilities
columns.description: Indicates if the cooking top has smart capabilities allowing it to connect to other devices or networks via different wireless protocols.

columns.name: Low-Power Mode Energy Consumption for Cooking Top (kWh/year)
columns.description: The annual combined low-power mode energy consumption for the cooking top ETLP (kWh/year) based on measured test data.

columns.name: Depth (inches)
columns.description: The product depth (inches) excluding the handles or knobs.

columns.name: Installation Type
columns.description: Designates whether the product is portable, built-in or slide-in, or freestanding.

columns.name: Number of Cooking Zones
columns.description: Indicates the number of cooking zones available.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #6 Wattage (watts)
columns.description: The wattage of the sixth cooking zone.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #2 Wattage (watts)
columns.description: The wattage of the second cooking zone.

columns.name: Product Type
columns.description: Designates whether the product is considered a cooktop or a range.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #5 Wattage (watts)
columns.description: The wattage of the fifth cooking zone.

columns.name: Cooking Zone #1 Wattage (watts)
columns.description: The wattage of the first cooking zone.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 1.0 ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Residential Electric Cooking Products that are effective as of September 25, 2023. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/electric_cooking_products/key_product_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Dehumidifiers

columns.name: Whole-home Dehumidifier Case Volume (cu. ft.)
columns.description: For whole-home dehumidifiers, this is a measure of the rectangular volume that the product case occupies, exclusive of any duct attachment collars or other external components.

columns.name: Alternate Configuration Type
columns.description: Some dehumidifiers can operate in both the portable and whole-home configurations by means of a ducting kit. For a dehumidifier sold as a whole-home type that can also operate as a portable type, the portable configuration is listed here as the alternate, and vice versa.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Date Available on Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what you need in your home to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control, e.g. ‘WiFi’ or Zigbee.

columns.name: Refrigerant Charge (ounces)
columns.description: Indicates the amount of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: Refrigerant Type
columns.description: Indicates the type of refrigerant used in the model.

columns.name: Dehumidifier Efficiency (Integrated Energy Factor - L/kWh)
columns.description: A measure of energy efficiency of a dehumidifier that encompasses dehumidification mode and non-dehumidification mode energy consumption.

columns.name: Connected Capable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model is capable of connecting to networks.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. This column includes alternative means to identify the model/model family (e.g., additional model names, additional model numbers, retailer SKUs, product descriptions).

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Dehumidifier Type
columns.description: Portable dehumidifiers operate within the dehumidified space without the attachment of additional ducting. Whole-home dehumidifiers are installed with ducting to deliver return process air to the inlet and to supply dehumidified process air from the outlet to one or more locations in the dehumidified space. Some dehumidifiers can operate in both configurations by means of a ducting kit.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: Connected Features
columns.description: Describes which (if any) connected features the model is capable of.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Dehumidifier Water Removal Capacity (pints/day)
columns.description: A measure of a dehumidifier's ability to remove moisture from its surrounding atmosphere, measured in pints collected per 24 hours, based on DOE's Appendix X1 test method. This represents the expected performance in a basement for portable dehumidifiers and expected performance in the average home for whole-home dehumidifiers.

columns.name: Annual Energy Consumption (kWh/year)
columns.description: This is the integrated annual energy consumption of the dehumidifier under typical conditions, including the energy used in dehumidification modes, fan modes, and standby modes.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Refrigerant with GWP
columns.description: Refrigerants are substances that cool or heat and are often used in refrigeration and HVAC equipment. Many refrigerants are harmful atmospheric pollutants that have a high global warming potential (GWP). Lower-GWP alternative refrigerant options for dehumidifiers include R-452B, HFC-32, R-513A, R-454B, R-454A, R-454C, and HFO-1234yf.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 6.0 Requirements that are effective as of October 1, 2025. A detailed listing of key efficiency criteria are available at https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/dehumidifiers/key_efficiency_criteria

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Light Fixtures - Downlights

columns.name: Can Size(s) (inches)
columns.description: The size of the recessed opening in the ceiling or width of the fixture.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Total Light Output (lumens)
columns.description: The amount of light emitted by the light source or light fixture. Non directional fixtures (with removable light sources i.e. GU24 base bulbs) are measured at the light source and do not account for light lost from the fixture, while other fixtures meant to put light on a specific application (e.g. recessed downlights, under cabinet lights, accent lights, cove lights) are measured at the fixture level, only counting the light that escapes the fixture.

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Notes
columns.description: Indicates information on certification relevant to the consumer (i.e. product variations, unique issues concerning the model’s listing, etc.).

columns.name: Special Features
columns.description: Most fixtures are designed to operate with an on/off switch. However, some fixtures have been designed with features to perform in specialized applications, such as with dimmers or with the ability to tune the color temperature to a warmer or cooler setting or to create different colors of lighting. Special features and the compatibility with controls should be clearly marked on the product packaging, cut sheet or website for the product.

columns.name: Power Factor
columns.description: The ratio of real power that flows to the light source versus the amount of power actually consumed. A light fixture with a lower power factor will draw more current than a fixture with a higher power factor for the same amount of power consumed.

columns.name: Light Source Connection/Base Type
columns.description: Downlight retrofit kits have a fully integrated light source but connect electrically to the existing socket (e.g., Edison base E26, GU24) or other method.

columns.name: Can Rating(s)
columns.description: Indicates if the recessed downlight fixture is rated for insulation contact (IC) or airtight (AT).

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what network you need to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control, e.g. ‘WiFi’ or Zigbee.

columns.name: Connected Capable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model meets optional connected criteria as defined in the specification, which may include limited network standby power and energy reporting.

columns.name: Light Source Life (hours)
columns.description: The expected useful life of the light source. Measured differently depending on the technology of the light source. For example, LED lighting expected useful life is based on the estimated point at which only 70% of the original light output remains, while for older technologies, like fluorescent and halogen, estimated useful life is based on the measured median sample life.

columns.name: Color Quality (CRI)
columns.description: The Color Rendering Index (CRI) of a light source represents the degree of color shift objects undergo when illuminated by the light source as compared with the color of those same objects when illuminated by a reference source of comparable color temperature. The ENERGY STAR specifications require a CRI of at least 80. Incandescent lighting has a CRI of 100. Early fluorescent lighting had CRIs in the 60s, resulting in unpleasant appearance of skin tones.

columns.name: Appearance/Correlated Color Temperature (Kelvin)
columns.description: A description of the "color" of a light source measured on the Kelvin (K) scale. CFLs are available in a range of color temperatures. Lower values (2700-3000K) mean the white light has a warmer (red-yellow) tone, while higher kelvin (4100-6500K) mean the white light has a cooler (bluer) tone.

columns.name: Energy Efficiency (lumens/Watt)
columns.description: The quotient of total light output (lumens) divided by total input power (Watts) expressed in lumens per watt (lm/W). This is roughly analagous to the gas mileage (i.e., miles per gallon) of your car.

columns.name: Total Input Power (Watts)
columns.description: Total power consumed by the luminaire while in use.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a  product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: A distinguishing, alphanumeric identifier, assigned to a product by the manufacturer or private labeler. The model number may include a wildcard that can be replaced by any letter or digit and still accurately capture what is covered by the model’s ENERGY STAR certification; an asterisk symbol (*) represents a letter, and a number symbol (#) a digit.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Brand Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Partner
columns.description: An organization that signed a Partnership Agreement with EPA to manufacture or private label ENERGY STAR qualified products.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Network Security Standards
columns.description: Describes the standards implemented in the product that provide privacy best practices and additional security by preventing or detecting network compromises.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

name: ENERGY STAR Certified Room Air Cleaners V3.0

columns.name: Dust-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate (cfm)
columns.description: The Rated Dust-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate or Dust CADR is the manufacturer-reported delivery of dust-free air produced by a household electric, cord-connected room air cleaner.

columns.name: Replacement Model Number for Filter #1
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a replacement filter by the manufacturer.

columns.name: Filter #3 Type
columns.description: Indicates the third type of filter, if the air cleaner has more than two filters. High-Efficiency Particulate Air filters or HEPA filters sometimes, also called True HEPA filters, have a minimum fractional particle removal efficiency of 99.97% for all particles of 0.3 μm diameter. Filters described as HEPA-Type or something other than “HEPA” or “True HEPA” have not demonstrated they meet the HEPA standard. Consumers should look to the product literature for documentation of the efficacy of these filters.

columns.name: Filter #4 Type
columns.description: Indicates the fourth type of filter, if the air cleaner has more than three filters. High-Efficiency Particulate Air filters or HEPA filters sometimes, also called True HEPA filters, have a minimum fractional particle removal efficiency of 99.97% for all particles of 0.3 μm diameter. Filters described as HEPA-Type or something other than “HEPA” or “True HEPA” have not demonstrated they meet the HEPA standard. Consumers should look to the product literature for documentation of the efficacy of these filters.

columns.name: Connected Capable
columns.description: Indicates whether the model is capable of connecting to networks.

columns.name: Connected Features
columns.description: Describes which (if any) connected features the model is capable of.

columns.name: Replacement Model Number for Filter #2
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a replacement filter by the manufacturer.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description: The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Model Name
columns.description: An identifier assigned by the manufacturer or private labeler to a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes.

columns.name: Annual Energy Use (kWh/yr)
columns.description: This is the annual energy consumption of the room air cleaner under typical conditions expressed in kWh/year. This figure assumes 16 hours of active mode operation per day.

columns.name: Technology Types
columns.description: Indicates the air cleaning technologies used by the room air cleaner, such as, fan and filter, fan with electrostatic plates, ion generator, and other technologies.

columns.name: PM2.5-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate (cfm)
columns.description: This indicates how quickly the air cleaner can remove particulate matter. Air purifiers with higher CADR ratings are able to clean the air more quickly than models with a lower CADR rating, and therefore are typically more appropriate for larger rooms. Please see the Room Size column for the appropriate room size for each model.

columns.name: Replacement Model Number for Filter #4
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a replacement filter by the manufacturer.

columns.name: Replacement Model Number for Filter #3
columns.description: A distinguishing identifier, usually alphanumeric, assigned to a replacement filter by the manufacturer.

columns.name: Connects Using
columns.description: Provides guidance about what you need in your home to enable connected capabilities like smart phone control, e.g. ‘WiFi’ or Zigbee.

columns.name: Filter #2 Type
columns.description: Indicates the secondary type of filter, if the air cleaner has more than one filter. High-Efficiency Particulate Air filters or HEPA filters sometimes, also called True HEPA filters, have a minimum fractional particle removal efficiency of 99.97% for all particles of 0.3 μm diameter. Filters described as HEPA-Type or something other than “HEPA” or “True HEPA” have not demonstrated they meet the HEPA standard. Consumers should look to the product literature for documentation of the efficacy of these filters.

columns.name: Filter #1 Type
columns.description: Indicates the primary type of filter, if the air cleaner has a filter. High-Efficiency Particulate Air filters or HEPA filters sometimes, also called True HEPA filters, have a minimum fractional particle removal efficiency of 99.97% for all particles of 0.3 μm diameter. Filters described as HEPA-Type or something other than “HEPA” or “True HEPA” have not demonstrated they meet the HEPA standard. Consumers should look to the product literature for documentation of the efficacy of these filters.

columns.name: Automatic Mode
columns.description: Indicates if the product has an automatic mode and if so, the type of automatic mode. An automatic mode is a mode in which the air cleaner has been activated and is performing in response to a sensor input, timer, or scheduling feature.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: CB Model Identifier
columns.description: A unique string of characters assigned by certification bodies (CBs) to identify a model or set of models with the same performance characteristics. This identifier should remain the same for a model even if it is recertified to a new version of an ENERGY STAR specification. This string of characters is determined by CBs and is not the ENERGY STAR Unique ID assigned by EPA.

columns.name: Integrated Energy Factor (cfm/Watt)
columns.description: The Integrated Energy Factor (IEF) is an energy efficiency metric for room air cleaners that incorporates the standby mode energy, as well as its active mode energy. More efficient units have a higher Integrated Energy Factor (cfm/Watt).

columns.name: Ozone Emissions (ppb)
columns.description: The ozone emissions, measured in parts per billion.

columns.name: Meets ENERGY STAR Most Efficient 2025 Criteria
columns.description: Indicates models meeting the Most Efficient criteria. The ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria recognizes the most efficient products among those that qualify for the ENERGY STAR. These exceptional products represent the leading edge in energy efficient products this year. For more information, please visit www.energystar.gov/mostefficient

columns.name: Markets
columns.description: Includes products sold in the U.S. and/or Canada and other ENERGY STAR partner countries.

columns.name: Room Size (sq.ft.)
columns.description: Room size in square feet is calculated by multiplying the Smoke CADR by 1.55 or by 0.144 for square meters. This room size formula is based on the CADR requirement to remove 80% of smoke particles, assuming one room air exchange per hour.

columns.name: Pollen-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate per Watt (cfm/Watt)
columns.description: The Pollen-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate per Watt or Pollen CADR/W is the energy efficiency metric for room air cleaners. More efficient units have higher Pollen CADR/W.

columns.name: Smoke-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate per Watt (cfm/Watt)
columns.description: The Smoke-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate per Watt or Smoke CADR/W is the energy efficiency metric for room air cleaners. More efficient units have higher Smoke CADR/W.

columns.name: Dust-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate per Watt (cfm/Watt)
columns.description: The Dust-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate per Watt or Dust CADR/W is the energy efficiency metric for room air cleaners. More efficient units have higher Dust CADR/W.

columns.name: Pollen-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate (cfm)
columns.description: The Rated Pollen-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate or Pollen CADR is the manufacturer-reported delivery of pollen-free air produced by a household electric, cord-connected room air cleaner.

columns.name: Smoke-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate (cfm)
columns.description: The Rated Smoke-Free Clean Air Delivery Rate or Smoke CADR is the manufacturer-reported delivery of smoke-free air produced by a household electric, cord-connected room air cleaner.

columns.name: Type
columns.description:
Desktop: A computer whose main unit is designed to be located in a permanent location, often on a desk or on the floor. Desktop computers are not designed for portability and are designed for use with an external display, keyboard, and mouse.

Integrated Computer: A desktop computer in which the computing hardware and display are integrated into a single housing, and which is connected to ac mains power through a single cable. Integrated desktop computers come in one of two possible forms: (1) a system where the display and computer are physically combined into a single unit; or (2) a system packaged as a single system where the display is separate but is connected to the main chassis by a dc power cord and both the computer and display are powered from a single power supply.

Notebook: A computer designed specifically for portability and to be operated for extended periods of time both with and without a direct connection to an ac mains power source.

Slate/tablet: A computing device designed for portability.

Portable All-in-one Computer: A computing device designed for limited portability.

Small-scale Server: A computer that typically uses desktop components in a desktop form factor, but is designed primarily to be a storage host for other computers. Small-scale Servers are designed to perform functions such as providing network infrastructure services (e.g., archiving) and hosting data/media.

Thin Client: An independently-powered computer that relies on a connection to remote computing resources to obtain primary functionality. Main computing functions (e.g., program execution, data storage, interaction with other Internet resources) are provided by the remote computing resources.

Workstation: A high-performance, single-user computer typically used for graphics, CAD, software development, financial and scientific applications among other compute intensive tasks.

columns.name: UPC
columns.description: UPC codes provided by partners for ENERGY STAR certified products. The brand, model name and model number continue to serve as the identifiers used to establish certification. The UPC code data below is intended to aid in identification of ENERGY STAR models. UPC code data is not provided for all certified models.

columns.name: Additional Model Information
columns.description: This column includes for the qualified model or family, family members, additional model names, model numbers and other identifying information associated with a product or family/series of products for sales and marketing purposes. Other identifying information includes, but is not limited to, SKUs, retail numbers, and/or descriptions of models included/not included in the reported Model Family.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Unique ID
columns.description:
The ENERGY STAR Unique ID (ESUID) is a string of
seven numbers EPA assigns to an ENERGY STAR model or set of models with unique
performance characteristics. This ESUID is unique to both the model and product
specification version and is assigned by EPA upon receipt of certification
information from the certification body. Each row within the product list will
have a unique ESUID. Developers may wish to use this ESUID to track information
on certified models in their information systems.

columns.name: Energy Efficient Ethernet (EEE)
columns.description: Provides whether the model has Energy Efficient Ethernet.

columns.name: 10 Gigabit Ethernet Ports (count)
columns.description: Indicates whether the model contains one or more 10 Gigabit (10 Gb/s) Ethernet ports.

columns.name: External Power Supply Rated Power (W)
columns.description: Required for models with an external power supply. Provide the nameplate output power which is the power output of the power supply as specified on the manufacturer's label on the power supply housing or, if absent from the housing, as specified in documentation provided by the manufacturer.

columns.name: Internal Power Supply Rated Power (W)
columns.description: Provides the nameplate output power which is the power output of the power supply as specified on the manufacturer's label on the power supply housing or, if absent from the housing, as specified in documentation provided by the manufacturer.

columns.name: Total Battery Capacity (watt-hours)
columns.description: Indicates the battery capacity of the product in watt-hours.

columns.name: Default Low-power Mode
columns.description: User-activated Low-power Mode Enabled by Default + User-activated Low-power Mode Enabled by Default - Other.

columns.name: Sleep Mode (watts)
columns.description: A low power mode automatically entered after a period of inactivity or by manual selection.

columns.name: Short Idle (watts)
columns.description: The power state in which the operating system and other software have completed loading, and the Computer has reached an Idle condition, but the Display is on, and Long Idle power management features have not engaged.

columns.name: Functional Adder Allowances (kWh)
columns.description: The energy allowance provided by the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: TEC of Model (kWh)
columns.description: Typical energy consumption of the model configuration tested as meeting Category 0 requirements.

columns.name: Date Available On Market
columns.description: The date that the model is available for purchase.

columns.name: Off Mode (watts)
columns.description: The lowest power mode which cannot be switched off.

columns.name: Date Certified
columns.description: The date on which the product was confirmed to meet the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Long Idle (watts)
columns.description: The power state in which the operating system and other software have completed loading, and the Computer has reached an Idle condition and the Display has entered a low-power state.

columns.name: Additional Measured Power in Short Idle at 115V (W)
columns.description: Provides the additional measured power in short idle at 115V.

columns.name: Base TEC Allowance (kWh)
columns.description: The energy allowance provided by the ENERGY STAR specification.

columns.name: Ethernet Capability
columns.description: Does the product have an ethernet port?

columns.name: Touch Screen
columns.description: If "Yes", the model includes a touch screen.

columns.name: Sleep Mode Default Time Upon Shipment (min.)
columns.description: Sleep Mode shall be set to activate after no more than 30 minutes of user inactivity.

description: Certified models meet all ENERGY STAR requirements as listed in the Version 9.0 Requirements that are effective as of October 27, 2025.
